26// start catch_suppress_warnings.h
27
28#ifdef __clang__
29# ifdef __ICC // icpc defines the __clang__ macro
30# pragma warning(push)
31# pragma warning(disable: 161 1682)
32# else // __ICC
33# pragma clang diagnostic push
34# pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wpadded"
35# pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wswitch-enum"
36# pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wcovered-switch-default"
37# endif
38#elif defined __GNUC__
39 // GCC likes to warn on REQUIREs, and we cannot suppress them
40 // locally because g++'s support for _Pragma is lacking in older,
41 // still supported, versions
42# pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wparentheses"
43# pragma GCC diagnostic push
44# pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-variable"
45# pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wpadded"
46#endif
47// end catch_suppress_warnings.h
48#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_MAIN) || defined(CATCH_CONFIG_RUNNER)
49# define CATCH_IMPL
50# define CATCH_CONFIG_ALL_PARTS
51#endif

53// In the impl file, we want to have access to all parts of the headers

55#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_ALL_PARTS)
56# define CATCH_CONFIG_EXTERNAL_INTERFACES
57# if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_MATCHERS)
58# undef CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_MATCHERS

62# endif

101// start catch_common.h
102
103// start catch_compiler_capabilities.h
104
105// Detect a number of compiler features - by compiler
106// The following features are defined:
107//
108// CATCH_CONFIG_COUNTER : is the __COUNTER__ macro supported?
109// CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH : is Windows SEH supported?
110// CATCH_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS : are POSIX signals supported?
111// ****************
112// Note to maintainers: if new toggles are added please document them
113// in configuration.md, too
114// ****************
115
116// In general each macro has a _NO_<feature name> form
117// (e.g. CATCH_CONFIG_NO_POSIX_SIGNALS) which disables the feature.
118// Many features, at point of detection, define an _INTERNAL_ macro, so they
119// can be combined, en-mass, with the _NO_ forms later.
120
121#ifdef __cplusplus
122
123# if __cplusplus >= 201402L
124# define CATCH_CPP14_OR_GREATER
125# endif
126
127# if __cplusplus >= 201703L
128# define CATCH_CPP17_OR_GREATER
129# endif
130
131#endif
132
133#if defined(CATCH_CPP17_OR_GREATER)
134# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_CPP17_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTIONS
135#endif
136
137#ifdef __clang__
138
139# define CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
140 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic push" ) \
141 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wexit-time-destructors\"" ) \
142 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wglobal-constructors\"")
143# define CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
144 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic pop" )
145
146# define CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS \
147 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic push" ) \
148 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wparentheses\"" )
149# define CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS \
150 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic pop" )
151
152# define CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS \
153 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic push" ) \
154 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wunused-variable\"" )
155# define CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS \
156 _Pragma( "clang diagnostic pop" )
157
158#endif // __clang__
159
161// Assume that non-Windows platforms support posix signals by default
162#if !defined(CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS)
163 #define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS
164#endif
165
167// We know some environments not to support full POSIX signals
168#if defined(__CYGWIN__) || defined(__QNX__) || defined(__EMSCRIPTEN__) || defined(__DJGPP__)
169 #define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_POSIX_SIGNALS
170#endif
171
172#ifdef __OS400__
173# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_POSIX_SIGNALS
174# define CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_NONE
175#endif
176
178// Android somehow still does not support std::to_string
179#if defined(__ANDROID__)
180# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_CPP11_TO_STRING
181#endif
182
184// Not all Windows environments support SEH properly
185#if defined(__MINGW32__)
186# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_WINDOWS_SEH
187#endif
188
190// PS4
191#if defined(__ORBIS__)
192# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_NEW_CAPTURE
193#endif
194
196// Cygwin
197#ifdef __CYGWIN__
198
199// Required for some versions of Cygwin to declare gettimeofday
200// see: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/36901803/gettimeofday-not-declared-in-this-scope-cygwin
201# define _BSD_SOURCE
202
203#endif // __CYGWIN__
204
206// Visual C++
207#ifdef _MSC_VER
208
209# if _MSC_VER >= 1900 // Visual Studio 2015 or newer
210# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_CPP17_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTIONS
211# endif
212
213// Universal Windows platform does not support SEH
214// Or console colours (or console at all...)
215# if defined(WINAPI_FAMILY) && (WINAPI_FAMILY == WINAPI_FAMILY_APP)
216# define CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_NONE
217# else
218# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH
219# endif
220
221#endif // _MSC_VER
222
224
225// DJGPP
226#ifdef __DJGPP__
227# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_WCHAR
228#endif // __DJGPP__
229
231
232// Use of __COUNTER__ is suppressed during code analysis in
233// CLion/AppCode 2017.2.x and former, because __COUNTER__ is not properly
234// handled by it.
235// Otherwise all supported compilers support COUNTER macro,
236// but user still might want to turn it off
237#if ( !defined(__JETBRAINS_IDE__) || __JETBRAINS_IDE__ >= 20170300L )
238 #define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_COUNTER
239#endif
240
241#if defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_COUNTER) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_COUNTER) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_COUNTER)
242# define CATCH_CONFIG_COUNTER
243#endif
244#if defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_WINDOWS_SEH) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH) && !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_WINDOWS_SEH)
245# define CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH
246#endif
247// This is set by default, because we assume that unix compilers are posix-signal-compatible by default.
248#if defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS) && !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_POSIX_SIGNALS) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_POSIX_SIGNALS) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS)
249# define CATCH_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS
250#endif
251// This is set by default, because we assume that compilers with no wchar_t support are just rare exceptions.
252#if !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_WCHAR) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_WCHAR) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_WCHAR)
253# define CATCH_CONFIG_WCHAR
254#endif
255
256#if !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_CPP11_TO_STRING) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_CPP11_TO_STRING) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_CPP11_TO_STRING)
257# define CATCH_CONFIG_CPP11_TO_STRING
258#endif
259
260#if defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_CPP17_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTIONS) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_CPP17_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTIONS) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_CPP17_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTIONS)
261# define CATCH_CONFIG_CPP17_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTIONS
262#endif
263
264#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_EXPERIMENTAL_REDIRECT)
265# define CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NEW_CAPTURE
266#endif
267
268#if defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NEW_CAPTURE) && !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_CONFIG_NO_NEW_CAPTURE) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NO_NEW_CAPTURE) && !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_NEW_CAPTURE)
269# define CATCH_CONFIG_NEW_CAPTURE
270#endif
271
272#if !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS)
273# define CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS
274# define CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS
275#endif
276#if !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS)
277# define CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS
278# define CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS
279#endif
280#if !defined(CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS)
281# define CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS
282# define CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS
283#endif
284
285// end catch_compiler_capabilities.h
286#define INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME_LINE2( name, line ) name##line
287#define INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME_LINE( name, line ) INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME_LINE2( name, line )
288#ifdef CATCH_CONFIG_COUNTER
289# define INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( name ) INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME_LINE( name, __COUNTER__ )
290#else
291# define INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( name ) INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME_LINE( name, __LINE__ )
292#endif

298namespace Catch {
299
300 struct CaseSensitive { enum Choice {
301 Yes,
302 No
303 }; };
304
305 class NonCopyable {
306 NonCopyable( NonCopyable const& ) = delete;
307 NonCopyable( NonCopyable && ) = delete;
308 NonCopyable& operator = ( NonCopyable const& ) = delete;
309 NonCopyable& operator = ( NonCopyable && ) = delete;
310
311 protected:
312 NonCopyable();
313 virtual ~NonCopyable();
314 };
315
316 struct SourceLineInfo {
317
318 SourceLineInfo() = delete;
319 SourceLineInfo( char const* _file, std::size_t _line ) noexcept
320 : file( _file ),
321 line( _line )
322 {}
323
324 SourceLineInfo( SourceLineInfo const& other ) = default;
325 SourceLineInfo( SourceLineInfo && ) = default;
326 SourceLineInfo& operator = ( SourceLineInfo const& ) = default;
327 SourceLineInfo& operator = ( SourceLineInfo && ) = default;
328
329 bool empty() const noexcept;
330 bool operator == ( SourceLineInfo const& other ) const noexcept;
331 bool operator < ( SourceLineInfo const& other ) const noexcept;
332
333 char const* file;
334 std::size_t line;
335 };
336
337 std::ostream& operator << ( std::ostream& os, SourceLineInfo const& info );
338
339 // Use this in variadic streaming macros to allow
340 // >> +StreamEndStop
341 // as well as
342 // >> stuff +StreamEndStop
343 struct StreamEndStop {
344 std::string operator+() const;
345 };
346 template<typename T>
347 T const& operator + ( T const& value, StreamEndStop ) {
348 return value;
349 }
350}
351
352#define CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO \
353 ::Catch::SourceLineInfo( __FILE__, static_cast<std::size_t>( __LINE__ ) )
354
355// end catch_common.h
356namespace Catch {
357
358 struct RegistrarForTagAliases {
359 RegistrarForTagAliases( char const* alias, char const* tag, SourceLineInfo const& lineInfo );
360 };
361
362} // end namespace Catch
363
364#define CATCH_REGISTER_TAG_ALIAS( alias, spec ) \
365 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
366 namespace{ Catch::RegistrarForTagAliases INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( AutoRegisterTagAlias )( alias, spec, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO ); } \
367 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS
368
369// end catch_tag_alias_autoregistrar.h

372// start catch_interfaces_testcase.h
373
374#include <vector>
375#include <memory>
376
377namespace Catch {
378
379 class TestSpec;
380
381 struct ITestInvoker {
382 virtual void invoke () const = 0;
383 virtual ~ITestInvoker();
384 };
385
386 using ITestCasePtr = std::shared_ptr<ITestInvoker>;
387
388 class TestCase;
389 struct IConfig;
390
391 struct ITestCaseRegistry {
392 virtual ~ITestCaseRegistry();
393 virtual std::vector<TestCase> const& getAllTests() const = 0;
394 virtual std::vector<TestCase> const& getAllTestsSorted( IConfig const& config ) const = 0;
395 };
396
397 bool matchTest( TestCase const& testCase, TestSpec const& testSpec, IConfig const& config );
398 std::vector<TestCase> filterTests( std::vector<TestCase> const& testCases, TestSpec const& testSpec, IConfig const& config );
399 std::vector<TestCase> const& getAllTestCasesSorted( IConfig const& config );
400
401}
402
403// end catch_interfaces_testcase.h
404// start catch_stringref.h
405
406#include <cstddef>
407#include <string>
408#include <iosfwd>
409
410namespace Catch {
411
412 class StringData;
413
421 class StringRef {
422 public:
423 using size_type = std::size_t;
424
425 private:
426 friend struct StringRefTestAccess;
427
428 char const* m_start;
429 size_type m_size;
430
431 char* m_data = nullptr;
432
433 void takeOwnership();
434
435 static constexpr char const* const s_empty = "";
436
437 public: // construction/ assignment
438 StringRef() noexcept
439 : StringRef( s_empty, 0 )
440 {}
441
442 StringRef( StringRef const& other ) noexcept
443 : m_start( other.m_start ),
444 m_size( other.m_size )
445 {}
446
447 StringRef( StringRef&& other ) noexcept
448 : m_start( other.m_start ),
449 m_size( other.m_size ),
450 m_data( other.m_data )
451 {
452 other.m_data = nullptr;
453 }
454
455 StringRef( char const* rawChars ) noexcept;
456
457 StringRef( char const* rawChars, size_type size ) noexcept
458 : m_start( rawChars ),
459 m_size( size )
460 {}
461
462 StringRef( std::string const& stdString ) noexcept
463 : m_start( stdString.c_str() ),
464 m_size( stdString.size() )
465 {}
466
467 ~StringRef() noexcept {
468 delete[] m_data;
469 }
470
471 auto operator = ( StringRef const &other ) noexcept -> StringRef& {
472 delete[] m_data;
473 m_data = nullptr;
474 m_start = other.m_start;
475 m_size = other.m_size;
476 return *this;
477 }
478
479 operator std::string() const;
480
481 void swap( StringRef& other ) noexcept;
482
483 public: // operators
484 auto operator == ( StringRef const& other ) const noexcept -> bool;
485 auto operator != ( StringRef const& other ) const noexcept -> bool;
486
487 auto operator[] ( size_type index ) const noexcept -> char;
488
489 public: // named queries
490 auto empty() const noexcept -> bool {
491 return m_size == 0;
492 }
493 auto size() const noexcept -> size_type {
494 return m_size;
495 }
496
497 auto numberOfCharacters() const noexcept -> size_type;
498 auto c_str() const -> char const*;
499
500 public: // substrings and searches
501 auto substr( size_type start, size_type size ) const noexcept -> StringRef;
502
503 // Returns the current start pointer.
504 // Note that the pointer can change when if the StringRef is a substring
505 auto currentData() const noexcept -> char const*;
506
507 private: // ownership queries - may not be consistent between calls
508 auto isOwned() const noexcept -> bool;
509 auto isSubstring() const noexcept -> bool;
510 };
511
512 auto operator + ( StringRef const& lhs, StringRef const& rhs ) -> std::string;
513 auto operator + ( StringRef const& lhs, char const* rhs ) -> std::string;
514 auto operator + ( char const* lhs, StringRef const& rhs ) -> std::string;
515
516 auto operator += ( std::string& lhs, StringRef const& sr ) -> std::string&;
517 auto operator << ( std::ostream& os, StringRef const& sr ) -> std::ostream&;
518
519 inline auto operator "" _sr( char const* rawChars, std::size_t size ) noexcept -> StringRef {
520 return StringRef( rawChars, size );
521 }
522
523} // namespace Catch
524
525// end catch_stringref.h
526namespace Catch {
527
528template<typename C>
529class TestInvokerAsMethod : public ITestInvoker {
530 void (C::*m_testAsMethod)();
531public:
532 TestInvokerAsMethod( void (C::*testAsMethod)() ) noexcept : m_testAsMethod( testAsMethod ) {}
533
534 void invoke() const override {
535 C obj;
536 (obj.*m_testAsMethod)();
537 }
538};
539
540auto makeTestInvoker( void(*testAsFunction)() ) noexcept -> ITestInvoker*;
541
542template<typename C>
543auto makeTestInvoker( void (C::*testAsMethod)() ) noexcept -> ITestInvoker* {
544 return new(std::nothrow) TestInvokerAsMethod<C>( testAsMethod );
545}
546
547struct NameAndTags {
548 NameAndTags( StringRef const& name_ = StringRef(), StringRef const& tags_ = StringRef() ) noexcept;
549 StringRef name;
550 StringRef tags;
551};
552
553struct AutoReg : NonCopyable {
554 AutoReg( ITestInvoker* invoker, SourceLineInfo const& lineInfo, StringRef const& classOrMethod, NameAndTags const& nameAndTags ) noexcept;
555 ~AutoReg();
556};
557
558} // end namespace Catch
559
560#define INTERNAL_CATCH_EXPAND1(param) INTERNAL_CATCH_EXPAND2(param)
561#define INTERNAL_CATCH_EXPAND2(...) INTERNAL_CATCH_NO## __VA_ARGS__
562#define INTERNAL_CATCH_DEF(...) INTERNAL_CATCH_DEF __VA_ARGS__
563#define INTERNAL_CATCH_NOINTERNAL_CATCH_DEF
564
565#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE)
566 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TESTCASE_NO_REGISTRATION( TestName, ... ) \
567 static void TestName()
568 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TESTCASE_METHOD_NO_REGISTRATION( TestName, ClassName, ... ) \
569 namespace{ \
570 struct TestName : INTERNAL_CATCH_EXPAND1(INTERNAL_CATCH_DEF ClassName) { \
571 void test(); \
572 }; \
573 } \
574 void TestName::test()
575
576#endif
577
579 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TESTCASE2( TestName, ... ) \
580 static void TestName(); \
581 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
582 namespace{ Catch::AutoReg INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( autoRegistrar )( Catch::makeTestInvoker( &TestName ), CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, Catch::StringRef(), Catch::NameAndTags{ __VA_ARGS__ } ); } /* NOLINT */ \
583 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
584 static void TestName()
585 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TESTCASE( ... ) \
586 INTERNAL_CATCH_TESTCASE2( INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( ____C_A_T_C_H____T_E_S_T____ ), __VA_ARGS__ )
587
589 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_METHOD_AS_TEST_CASE( QualifiedMethod, ... ) \
590 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
591 namespace{ Catch::AutoReg INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( autoRegistrar )( Catch::makeTestInvoker( &QualifiedMethod ), CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, "&" #QualifiedMethod, Catch::NameAndTags{ __VA_ARGS__ } ); } /* NOLINT */ \
592 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS
593
595 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TEST_CASE_METHOD2( TestName, ClassName, ... )\
596 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
597 namespace{ \
598 struct TestName : INTERNAL_CATCH_EXPAND1(INTERNAL_CATCH_DEF ClassName) { \
599 void test(); \
600 }; \
601 Catch::AutoReg INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( autoRegistrar ) ( Catch::makeTestInvoker( &TestName::test ), CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, #ClassName, Catch::NameAndTags{ __VA_ARGS__ } ); /* NOLINT */ \
602 } \
603 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
604 void TestName::test()
605 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TEST_CASE_METHOD( ClassName, ... ) \
606 INTERNAL_CATCH_TEST_CASE_METHOD2( INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( ____C_A_T_C_H____T_E_S_T____ ), ClassName, __VA_ARGS__ )
607
609 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_REGISTER_TESTCASE( Function, ... ) \
610 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
611 Catch::AutoReg INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( autoRegistrar )( Catch::makeTestInvoker( Function ), CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, Catch::StringRef(), Catch::NameAndTags{ __VA_ARGS__ } ); /* NOLINT */ \
612 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS
613
614// end catch_test_registry.h

621// start catch_result_type.h
622
623namespace Catch {
624
625 // ResultWas::OfType enum
626 struct ResultWas { enum OfType {
627 Unknown = -1,
628 Ok = 0,
629 Info = 1,
630 Warning = 2,
631
632 FailureBit = 0x10,
633
634 ExpressionFailed = FailureBit | 1,
635 ExplicitFailure = FailureBit | 2,
636
637 Exception = 0x100 | FailureBit,
638
639 ThrewException = Exception | 1,
640 DidntThrowException = Exception | 2,
641
642 FatalErrorCondition = 0x200 | FailureBit
643
644 }; };
645
646 bool isOk( ResultWas::OfType resultType );
647 bool isJustInfo( int flags );
648
649 // ResultDisposition::Flags enum
650 struct ResultDisposition { enum Flags {
651 Normal = 0x01,
652
653 ContinueOnFailure = 0x02, // Failures fail test, but execution continues
654 FalseTest = 0x04, // Prefix expression with !
655 SuppressFail = 0x08 // Failures are reported but do not fail the test
656 }; };
657
658 ResultDisposition::Flags operator | ( ResultDisposition::Flags lhs, ResultDisposition::Flags rhs );
659
660 bool shouldContinueOnFailure( int flags );
661 inline bool isFalseTest( int flags ) { return ( flags & ResultDisposition::FalseTest ) != 0; }
662 bool shouldSuppressFailure( int flags );
663
664} // end namespace Catch
665
666// end catch_result_type.h
667namespace Catch {
668
669 struct AssertionInfo
670 {
671 StringRef macroName;
672 SourceLineInfo lineInfo;
673 StringRef capturedExpression;
674 ResultDisposition::Flags resultDisposition;
675
676 // We want to delete this constructor but a compiler bug in 4.8 means
677 // the struct is then treated as non-aggregate
678 //AssertionInfo() = delete;
679 };
680
681} // end namespace Catch
682
683// end catch_assertioninfo.h
