692// start catch_stream.h
693
694#include <iosfwd>
695#include <cstddef>
696#include <ostream>
697
698namespace Catch {
699
700 std::ostream& cout();
701 std::ostream& cerr();
702 std::ostream& clog();
703
704 class StringRef;
705
706 struct IStream {
707 virtual ~IStream();
708 virtual std::ostream& stream() const = 0;
709 };
710
711 auto makeStream( StringRef const &filename ) -> IStream const*;
712
713 class ReusableStringStream {
714 std::size_t m_index;
715 std::ostream* m_oss;
716 public:
717 ReusableStringStream();
718 ~ReusableStringStream();
719
720 auto str() const -> std::string;
721
722 template<typename T>
723 auto operator << ( T const& value ) -> ReusableStringStream& {
724 *m_oss << value;
725 return *this;
726 }
727 auto get() -> std::ostream& { return *m_oss; }
728
729 static void cleanup();
730 };
731}
732
733// end catch_stream.h
734
735#ifdef __OBJC__
736// start catch_objc_arc.hpp
737
738#import <Foundation/Foundation.h>
739
740#ifdef __has_feature
741#define CATCH_ARC_ENABLED __has_feature(objc_arc)
742#else
743#define CATCH_ARC_ENABLED 0
744#endif
745
746void arcSafeRelease( NSObject* obj );
747id performOptionalSelector( id obj, SEL sel );
748
749#if !CATCH_ARC_ENABLED
750inline void arcSafeRelease( NSObject* obj ) {
751 [obj release];
752}
753inline id performOptionalSelector( id obj, SEL sel ) {
754 if( [obj respondsToSelector: sel] )
755 return [obj performSelector: sel];
756 return nil;
757}
758#define CATCH_UNSAFE_UNRETAINED
759#define CATCH_ARC_STRONG
760#else
761inline void arcSafeRelease( NSObject* ){}
762inline id performOptionalSelector( id obj, SEL sel ) {
763#ifdef __clang__
764#pragma clang diagnostic push
765#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Warc-performSelector-leaks"
766#endif
767 if( [obj respondsToSelector: sel] )
768 return [obj performSelector: sel];
769#ifdef __clang__
770#pragma clang diagnostic pop
771#endif
772 return nil;
773}
774#define CATCH_UNSAFE_UNRETAINED __unsafe_unretained
775#define CATCH_ARC_STRONG __strong
776#endif
777
778// end catch_objc_arc.hpp
779#endif
780
781#ifdef _MSC_VER
782#pragma warning(push)
783#pragma warning(disable:4180) // We attempt to stream a function (address) by const&, which MSVC complains about but is harmless
784#endif
785
786// We need a dummy global operator<< so we can bring it into Catch namespace later
787struct Catch_global_namespace_dummy {};
788std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&, Catch_global_namespace_dummy);
789
790namespace Catch {
791 // Bring in operator<< from global namespace into Catch namespace
792 using ::operator<<;
793
794 namespace Detail {
795
796 extern const std::string unprintableString;
797
798 std::string rawMemoryToString( const void *object, std::size_t size );
799
800 template<typename T>
801 std::string rawMemoryToString( const T& object ) {
802 return rawMemoryToString( &object, sizeof(object) );
803 }
804
805 template<typename T>
806 class IsStreamInsertable {
807 template<typename SS, typename TT>
808 static auto test(int)
809 -> decltype(std::declval<SS&>() << std::declval<TT>(), std::true_type());
810
811 template<typename, typename>
812 static auto test(...)->std::false_type;
813
814 public:
815 static const bool value = decltype(test<std::ostream, const T&>(0))::value;
816 };
817
818 template<typename E>
819 std::string convertUnknownEnumToString( E e );
820
821 template<typename T>
822 typename std::enable_if<
823 !std::is_enum<T>::value && !std::is_base_of<std::exception, T>::value,
824 std::string>::type convertUnstreamable( T const& ) {
825 return Detail::unprintableString;
826 }
827 template<typename T>
828 typename std::enable_if<
829 !std::is_enum<T>::value && std::is_base_of<std::exception, T>::value,
830 std::string>::type convertUnstreamable(T const& ex) {
831 return ex.what();
832 }
833
834 template<typename T>
835 typename std::enable_if<
836 std::is_enum<T>::value
837 , std::string>::type convertUnstreamable( T const& value ) {
838 return convertUnknownEnumToString( value );
839 }
840
841#if defined(_MANAGED)
843 template<typename T>
844 std::string clrReferenceToString( T^ ref ) {
845 if (ref == nullptr)
846 return std::string("null");
847 auto bytes = System::Text::Encoding::UTF8->GetBytes(ref->ToString());
848 cli::pin_ptr<System::Byte> p = &bytes[0];
849 return std::string(reinterpret_cast<char const *>(p), bytes->Length);
850 }
851#endif
852
853 } // namespace Detail
854
855 // If we decide for C++14, change these to enable_if_ts
856 template <typename T, typename = void>
857 struct StringMaker {
858 template <typename Fake = T>
859 static
860 typename std::enable_if<::Catch::Detail::IsStreamInsertable<Fake>::value, std::string>::type
861 convert(const Fake& value) {
862 ReusableStringStream rss;
863 // NB: call using the function-like syntax to avoid ambiguity with
864 // user-defined templated operator<< under clang.
865 rss.operator<<(value);
866 return rss.str();
867 }
868
869 template <typename Fake = T>
870 static
871 typename std::enable_if<!::Catch::Detail::IsStreamInsertable<Fake>::value, std::string>::type
872 convert( const Fake& value ) {
873#if !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_FALLBACK_STRINGIFIER)
874 return Detail::convertUnstreamable(value);
875#else
876 return CATCH_CONFIG_FALLBACK_STRINGIFIER(value);
877#endif
878 }
879 };
880
881 namespace Detail {
882
883 // This function dispatches all stringification requests inside of Catch.
884 // Should be preferably called fully qualified, like ::Catch::Detail::stringify
885 template <typename T>
886 std::string stringify(const T& e) {
887 return ::Catch::StringMaker<typename std::remove_cv<typename std::remove_reference<T>::type>::type>::convert(e);
888 }
889
890 template<typename E>
891 std::string convertUnknownEnumToString( E e ) {
892 return ::Catch::Detail::stringify(static_cast<typename std::underlying_type<E>::type>(e));
893 }
894
895#if defined(_MANAGED)
896 template <typename T>
897 std::string stringify( T^ e ) {
898 return ::Catch::StringMaker<T^>::convert(e);
899 }
900#endif
901
902 } // namespace Detail
903
904 // Some predefined specializations
905
906 template<>
907 struct StringMaker<std::string> {
908 static std::string convert(const std::string& str);
909 };
910#ifdef CATCH_CONFIG_WCHAR
911 template<>
912 struct StringMaker<std::wstring> {
913 static std::string convert(const std::wstring& wstr);
914 };
915#endif
916
917 template<>
918 struct StringMaker<char const *> {
919 static std::string convert(char const * str);
920 };
921 template<>
922 struct StringMaker<char *> {
923 static std::string convert(char * str);
924 };
925
926#ifdef CATCH_CONFIG_WCHAR
927 template<>
928 struct StringMaker<wchar_t const *> {
929 static std::string convert(wchar_t const * str);
930 };
931 template<>
932 struct StringMaker<wchar_t *> {
933 static std::string convert(wchar_t * str);
934 };
935#endif
936
937 // TBD: Should we use `strnlen` to ensure that we don't go out of the buffer,
938 // while keeping string semantics?
939 template<int SZ>
940 struct StringMaker<char[SZ]> {
941 static std::string convert(char const* str) {
942 return ::Catch::Detail::stringify(std::string{ str });
943 }
944 };
945 template<int SZ>
946 struct StringMaker<signed char[SZ]> {
947 static std::string convert(signed char const* str) {
948 return ::Catch::Detail::stringify(std::string{ reinterpret_cast<char const *>(str) });
949 }
950 };
951 template<int SZ>
952 struct StringMaker<unsigned char[SZ]> {
953 static std::string convert(unsigned char const* str) {
954 return ::Catch::Detail::stringify(std::string{ reinterpret_cast<char const *>(str) });
955 }
956 };
957
958 template<>
959 struct StringMaker<int> {
960 static std::string convert(int value);
961 };
962 template<>
963 struct StringMaker<long> {
964 static std::string convert(long value);
965 };
966 template<>
967 struct StringMaker<long long> {
968 static std::string convert(long long value);
969 };
970 template<>
971 struct StringMaker<unsigned int> {
972 static std::string convert(unsigned int value);
973 };
974 template<>
975 struct StringMaker<unsigned long> {
976 static std::string convert(unsigned long value);
977 };
978 template<>
979 struct StringMaker<unsigned long long> {
980 static std::string convert(unsigned long long value);
981 };
982
983 template<>
984 struct StringMaker<bool> {
985 static std::string convert(bool b);
986 };
987
988 template<>
989 struct StringMaker<char> {
990 static std::string convert(char c);
991 };
992 template<>
993 struct StringMaker<signed char> {
994 static std::string convert(signed char c);
995 };
996 template<>
997 struct StringMaker<unsigned char> {
998 static std::string convert(unsigned char c);
999 };
1000
1001 template<>
1002 struct StringMaker<std::nullptr_t> {
1003 static std::string convert(std::nullptr_t);
1004 };
1005
1006 template<>
1007 struct StringMaker<float> {
1008 static std::string convert(float value);
1009 };
1010 template<>
1011 struct StringMaker<double> {
1012 static std::string convert(double value);
1013 };
1014
1015 template <typename T>
1016 struct StringMaker<T*> {
1017 template <typename U>
1018 static std::string convert(U* p) {
1019 if (p) {
1020 return ::Catch::Detail::rawMemoryToString(p);
1021 } else {
1022 return "nullptr";
1023 }
1024 }
1025 };
1026
1027 template <typename R, typename C>
1028 struct StringMaker<R C::*> {
1029 static std::string convert(R C::* p) {
1030 if (p) {
1031 return ::Catch::Detail::rawMemoryToString(p);
1032 } else {
1033 return "nullptr";
1034 }
1035 }
1036 };
1037
1038#if defined(_MANAGED)
1039 template <typename T>
1040 struct StringMaker<T^> {
1041 static std::string convert( T^ ref ) {
1042 return ::Catch::Detail::clrReferenceToString(ref);
1043 }
1044 };
1045#endif
1046
1047 namespace Detail {
1048 template<typename InputIterator>
1049 std::string rangeToString(InputIterator first, InputIterator last) {
1050 ReusableStringStream rss;
1051 rss << "{ ";
1052 if (first != last) {
1053 rss << ::Catch::Detail::stringify(*first);
1054 for (++first; first != last; ++first)
1055 rss << ", " << ::Catch::Detail::stringify(*first);
1056 }
1057 rss << " }";
1058 return rss.str();
1059 }
1060 }
1061
1062#ifdef __OBJC__
1063 template<>
1064 struct StringMaker<NSString*> {
1065 static std::string convert(NSString * nsstring) {
1066 if (!nsstring)
1067 return "nil";
1068 return std::string("@") + [nsstring UTF8String];
1069 }
1070 };
1071 template<>
1072 struct StringMaker<NSObject*> {
1073 static std::string convert(NSObject* nsObject) {
1074 return ::Catch::Detail::stringify([nsObject description]);
1075 }
1076
1077 };
1078 namespace Detail {
1079 inline std::string stringify( NSString* nsstring ) {
1080 return StringMaker<NSString*>::convert( nsstring );
1081 }
1082
1083 } // namespace Detail
1084#endif // __OBJC__
1085
1086} // namespace Catch
1087
1089// Separate std-lib types stringification, so it can be selectively enabled
1090// This means that we do not bring in
1091
1092#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_ALL_STRINGMAKERS)
1093# define CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_PAIR_STRINGMAKER
1094# define CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_TUPLE_STRINGMAKER
1095# define CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_CHRONO_STRINGMAKER
1096#endif
1097
1098// Separate std::pair specialization
1099#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_PAIR_STRINGMAKER)
1100#include <utility>
1101namespace Catch {
1102 template<typename T1, typename T2>
1103 struct StringMaker<std::pair<T1, T2> > {
1104 static std::string convert(const std::pair<T1, T2>& pair) {
1105 ReusableStringStream rss;
1106 rss << "{ "
1107 << ::Catch::Detail::stringify(pair.first)
1108 << ", "
1109 << ::Catch::Detail::stringify(pair.second)
1110 << " }";
1111 return rss.str();
1112 }
1113 };
1114}
1115#endif // CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_PAIR_STRINGMAKER
1116
1117// Separate std::tuple specialization
1118#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_TUPLE_STRINGMAKER)
1119#include <tuple>
1120namespace Catch {
1121 namespace Detail {
1122 template<
1123 typename Tuple,
1124 std::size_t N = 0,
1125 bool = (N < std::tuple_size<Tuple>::value)
1126 >
1127 struct TupleElementPrinter {
1128 static void print(const Tuple& tuple, std::ostream& os) {
1129 os << (N ? ", " : " ")
1130 << ::Catch::Detail::stringify(std::get<N>(tuple));
1131 TupleElementPrinter<Tuple, N + 1>::print(tuple, os);
1132 }
1133 };
1134
1135 template<
1136 typename Tuple,
1137 std::size_t N
1138 >
1139 struct TupleElementPrinter<Tuple, N, false> {
1140 static void print(const Tuple&, std::ostream&) {}
1141 };
1142
1143 }
1144
1145 template<typename ...Types>
1146 struct StringMaker<std::tuple<Types...>> {
1147 static std::string convert(const std::tuple<Types...>& tuple) {
1148 ReusableStringStream rss;
1149 rss << '{';
1150 Detail::TupleElementPrinter<std::tuple<Types...>>::print(tuple, rss.get());
1151 rss << " }";
1152 return rss.str();
1153 }
1154 };
1155}
1156#endif // CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_TUPLE_STRINGMAKER
1157
1158namespace Catch {
1159 struct not_this_one {}; // Tag type for detecting which begin/ end are being selected
1160
1161 // Import begin/ end from std here so they are considered alongside the fallback (...) overloads in this namespace
1162 using std::begin;
1163 using std::end;
1164
1165 not_this_one begin( ... );
1166 not_this_one end( ... );
1167
1168 template <typename T>
1169 struct is_range {
1170 static const bool value =
1171 !std::is_same<decltype(begin(std::declval<T>())), not_this_one>::value &&
1172 !std::is_same<decltype(end(std::declval<T>())), not_this_one>::value;
1173 };
1174
1175#if defined(_MANAGED) // Managed types are never ranges
1176 template <typename T>
1177 struct is_range<T^> {
1178 static const bool value = false;
1179 };
1180#endif
1181
1182 template<typename Range>
1183 std::string rangeToString( Range const& range ) {
1184 return ::Catch::Detail::rangeToString( begin( range ), end( range ) );
1185 }
1186
1187 // Handle vector<bool> specially
1188 template<typename Allocator>
1189 std::string rangeToString( std::vector<bool, Allocator> const& v ) {
1190 ReusableStringStream rss;
1191 rss << "{ ";
1192 bool first = true;
1193 for( bool b : v ) {
1194 if( first )
1195 first = false;
1196 else
1197 rss << ", ";
1198 rss << ::Catch::Detail::stringify( b );
1199 }
1200 rss << " }";
1201 return rss.str();
1202 }
1203
1204 template<typename R>
1205 struct StringMaker<R, typename std::enable_if<is_range<R>::value && !::Catch::Detail::IsStreamInsertable<R>::value>::type> {
1206 static std::string convert( R const& range ) {
1207 return rangeToString( range );
1208 }
1209 };
1210
1211 template <typename T, int SZ>
1212 struct StringMaker<T[SZ]> {
1213 static std::string convert(T const(&arr)[SZ]) {
1214 return rangeToString(arr);
1215 }
1216 };
1217
1218} // namespace Catch
1219
1220// Separate std::chrono::duration specialization
1221#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_CHRONO_STRINGMAKER)
1222#include <ctime>
1223#include <ratio>
1224#include <chrono>
1225
1226namespace Catch {
1227
1228template <class Ratio>
1229struct ratio_string {
1230 static std::string symbol();
1231};
1232
1233template <class Ratio>
1234std::string ratio_string<Ratio>::symbol() {
1235 Catch::ReusableStringStream rss;
1236 rss << '[' << Ratio::num << '/'
1237 << Ratio::den << ']';
1238 return rss.str();
1239}
1240template <>
1241struct ratio_string<std::atto> {
1242 static std::string symbol();
1243};
1244template <>
1245struct ratio_string<std::femto> {
1246 static std::string symbol();
1247};
1248template <>
1249struct ratio_string<std::pico> {
1250 static std::string symbol();
1251};
1252template <>
1253struct ratio_string<std::nano> {
1254 static std::string symbol();
1255};
1256template <>
1257struct ratio_string<std::micro> {
1258 static std::string symbol();
1259};
1260template <>
1261struct ratio_string<std::milli> {
1262 static std::string symbol();
1263};
1264
1266 // std::chrono::duration specializations
1267 template<typename Value, typename Ratio>
1268 struct StringMaker<std::chrono::duration<Value, Ratio>> {
1269 static std::string convert(std::chrono::duration<Value, Ratio> const& duration) {
1270 ReusableStringStream rss;
1271 rss << duration.count() << ' ' << ratio_string<Ratio>::symbol() << 's';
1272 return rss.str();
1273 }
1274 };
1275 template<typename Value>
1276 struct StringMaker<std::chrono::duration<Value, std::ratio<1>>> {
1277 static std::string convert(std::chrono::duration<Value, std::ratio<1>> const& duration) {
1278 ReusableStringStream rss;
1279 rss << duration.count() << " s";
1280 return rss.str();
1281 }
1282 };
1283 template<typename Value>
1284 struct StringMaker<std::chrono::duration<Value, std::ratio<60>>> {
1285 static std::string convert(std::chrono::duration<Value, std::ratio<60>> const& duration) {
1286 ReusableStringStream rss;
1287 rss << duration.count() << " m";
1288 return rss.str();
1289 }
1290 };
1291 template<typename Value>
1292 struct StringMaker<std::chrono::duration<Value, std::ratio<3600>>> {
1293 static std::string convert(std::chrono::duration<Value, std::ratio<3600>> const& duration) {
1294 ReusableStringStream rss;
1295 rss << duration.count() << " h";
1296 return rss.str();
1297 }
1298 };
1299
1301 // std::chrono::time_point specialization
1302 // Generic time_point cannot be specialized, only std::chrono::time_point<system_clock>
1303 template<typename Clock, typename Duration>
1304 struct StringMaker<std::chrono::time_point<Clock, Duration>> {
1305 static std::string convert(std::chrono::time_point<Clock, Duration> const& time_point) {
1306 return ::Catch::Detail::stringify(time_point.time_since_epoch()) + " since epoch";
1307 }
1308 };
1309 // std::chrono::time_point<system_clock> specialization
1310 template<typename Duration>
1311 struct StringMaker<std::chrono::time_point<std::chrono::system_clock, Duration>> {
1312 static std::string convert(std::chrono::time_point<std::chrono::system_clock, Duration> const& time_point) {
1313 auto converted = std::chrono::system_clock::to_time_t(time_point);
1314
1315#ifdef _MSC_VER
1316 std::tm timeInfo = {};
1317 gmtime_s(&timeInfo, &converted);
1318#else
1319 std::tm* timeInfo = std::gmtime(&converted);
1320#endif
1321
1322 auto const timeStampSize = sizeof("2017-01-16T17:06:45Z");
1323 char timeStamp[timeStampSize];
1324 const char * const fmt = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ";
1325
1326#ifdef _MSC_VER
1327 std::strftime(timeStamp, timeStampSize, fmt, &timeInfo);
1328#else
1329 std::strftime(timeStamp, timeStampSize, fmt, timeInfo);
1330#endif
1331 return std::string(timeStamp);
1332 }
1333 };
1334}
1335#endif // CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_CHRONO_STRINGMAKER
1336
1337#ifdef _MSC_VER
1338#pragma warning(pop)
1339#endif
1340
1341// end catch_tostring.h
1342#include <iosfwd>
1343
1344#ifdef _MSC_VER
1345#pragma warning(push)
1346#pragma warning(disable:4389) // '==' : signed/unsigned mismatch
1347#pragma warning(disable:4018) // more "signed/unsigned mismatch"
1348#pragma warning(disable:4312) // Converting int to T* using reinterpret_cast (issue on x64 platform)
1349#pragma warning(disable:4180) // qualifier applied to function type has no meaning
1350#endif
1351
1352namespace Catch {
1353
1354 struct ITransientExpression {
1355 auto isBinaryExpression() const -> bool { return m_isBinaryExpression; }
1356 auto getResult() const -> bool { return m_result; }
1357 virtual void streamReconstructedExpression( std::ostream &os ) const = 0;
1358
1359 ITransientExpression( bool isBinaryExpression, bool result )
1360 : m_isBinaryExpression( isBinaryExpression ),
1361 m_result( result )
1362 {}
1363
1364 // We don't actually need a virtual destructor, but many static analysers
1365 // complain if it's not here :-(
1366 virtual ~ITransientExpression();
1367
1368 bool m_isBinaryExpression;
1369 bool m_result;
1370
1371 };
1372
1373 void formatReconstructedExpression( std::ostream &os, std::string const& lhs, StringRef op, std::string const& rhs );
1374
1375 template<typename LhsT, typename RhsT>
1376 class BinaryExpr : public ITransientExpression {
1377 LhsT m_lhs;
1378 StringRef m_op;
1379 RhsT m_rhs;
1380
1381 void streamReconstructedExpression( std::ostream &os ) const override {
1382 formatReconstructedExpression
1383 ( os, Catch::Detail::stringify( m_lhs ), m_op, Catch::Detail::stringify( m_rhs ) );
1384 }
1385
1386 public:
1387 BinaryExpr( bool comparisonResult, LhsT lhs, StringRef op, RhsT rhs )
1388 : ITransientExpression{ true, comparisonResult },
1389 m_lhs( lhs ),
1390 m_op( op ),
1391 m_rhs( rhs )
1392 {}
1393 };
1394
1395 template<typename LhsT>
1396 class UnaryExpr : public ITransientExpression {
1397 LhsT m_lhs;
1398
1399 void streamReconstructedExpression( std::ostream &os ) const override {
1400 os << Catch::Detail::stringify( m_lhs );
1401 }
1402
1403 public:
1404 explicit UnaryExpr( LhsT lhs )
1405 : ITransientExpression{ false, lhs ? true : false },
1406 m_lhs( lhs )
1407 {}
1408 };
1409
1410 // Specialised comparison functions to handle equality comparisons between ints and pointers (NULL deduces as an int)
1411 template<typename LhsT, typename RhsT>
1412 auto compareEqual( LhsT const& lhs, RhsT const& rhs ) -> bool { return static_cast<bool>(lhs == rhs); }
1413 template<typename T>
1414 auto compareEqual( T* const& lhs, int rhs ) -> bool { return lhs == reinterpret_cast<void const*>( rhs ); }
1415 template<typename T>
1416 auto compareEqual( T* const& lhs, long rhs ) -> bool { return lhs == reinterpret_cast<void const*>( rhs ); }
1417 template<typename T>
1418 auto compareEqual( int lhs, T* const& rhs ) -> bool { return reinterpret_cast<void const*>( lhs ) == rhs; }
1419 template<typename T>
1420 auto compareEqual( long lhs, T* const& rhs ) -> bool { return reinterpret_cast<void const*>( lhs ) == rhs; }
1421
1422 template<typename LhsT, typename RhsT>
1423 auto compareNotEqual( LhsT const& lhs, RhsT&& rhs ) -> bool { return static_cast<bool>(lhs != rhs); }
1424 template<typename T>
1425 auto compareNotEqual( T* const& lhs, int rhs ) -> bool { return lhs != reinterpret_cast<void const*>( rhs ); }
1426 template<typename T>
1427 auto compareNotEqual( T* const& lhs, long rhs ) -> bool { return lhs != reinterpret_cast<void const*>( rhs ); }
1428 template<typename T>
1429 auto compareNotEqual( int lhs, T* const& rhs ) -> bool { return reinterpret_cast<void const*>( lhs ) != rhs; }
1430 template<typename T>
1431 auto compareNotEqual( long lhs, T* const& rhs ) -> bool { return reinterpret_cast<void const*>( lhs ) != rhs; }
1432
1433 template<typename LhsT>
1434 class ExprLhs {
1435 LhsT m_lhs;
1436 public:
1437 explicit ExprLhs( LhsT lhs ) : m_lhs( lhs ) {}
1438
1439 template<typename RhsT>
1440 auto operator == ( RhsT const& rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, RhsT const&> const {
1441 return { compareEqual( m_lhs, rhs ), m_lhs, "==", rhs };
1442 }
1443 auto operator == ( bool rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, bool> const {
1444 return { m_lhs == rhs, m_lhs, "==", rhs };
1445 }
1446
1447 template<typename RhsT>
1448 auto operator != ( RhsT const& rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, RhsT const&> const {
1449 return { compareNotEqual( m_lhs, rhs ), m_lhs, "!=", rhs };
1450 }
1451 auto operator != ( bool rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, bool> const {
1452 return { m_lhs != rhs, m_lhs, "!=", rhs };
1453 }
1454
1455 template<typename RhsT>
1456 auto operator > ( RhsT const& rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, RhsT const&> const {
1457 return { static_cast<bool>(m_lhs > rhs), m_lhs, ">", rhs };
1458 }
1459 template<typename RhsT>
1460 auto operator < ( RhsT const& rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, RhsT const&> const {
1461 return { static_cast<bool>(m_lhs < rhs), m_lhs, "<", rhs };
1462 }
1463 template<typename RhsT>
1464 auto operator >= ( RhsT const& rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, RhsT const&> const {
1465 return { static_cast<bool>(m_lhs >= rhs), m_lhs, ">=", rhs };
1466 }
1467 template<typename RhsT>
1468 auto operator <= ( RhsT const& rhs ) -> BinaryExpr<LhsT, RhsT const&> const {
1469 return { static_cast<bool>(m_lhs <= rhs), m_lhs, "<=", rhs };
1470 }
1471
1472 auto makeUnaryExpr() const -> UnaryExpr<LhsT> {
1473 return UnaryExpr<LhsT>{ m_lhs };
1474 }
1475 };
1476
1477 void handleExpression( ITransientExpression const& expr );
1478
1479 template<typename T>
1480 void handleExpression( ExprLhs<T> const& expr ) {
1481 handleExpression( expr.makeUnaryExpr() );
1482 }
1483
1484 struct Decomposer {
1485 template<typename T>
1486 auto operator <= ( T const& lhs ) -> ExprLhs<T const&> {
1487 return ExprLhs<T const&>{ lhs };
1488 }
1489
1490 auto operator <=( bool value ) -> ExprLhs<bool> {
1491 return ExprLhs<bool>{ value };
1492 }
1493 };
1494
1495} // end namespace Catch
1496
1497#ifdef _MSC_VER
1498#pragma warning(pop)
1499#endif
1500
1501// end catch_decomposer.h
1502// start catch_interfaces_capture.h
1503
1504#include <string>
1505
1506namespace Catch {
1507
1508 class AssertionResult;
1509 struct AssertionInfo;
1510 struct SectionInfo;
1511 struct SectionEndInfo;
1512 struct MessageInfo;
1513 struct Counts;
1514 struct BenchmarkInfo;
1515 struct BenchmarkStats;
1516 struct AssertionReaction;
1517
1518 struct ITransientExpression;
1519
1520 struct IResultCapture {
1521
1522 virtual ~IResultCapture();
1523
1524 virtual bool sectionStarted( SectionInfo const& sectionInfo,
1525 Counts& assertions ) = 0;
1526 virtual void sectionEnded( SectionEndInfo const& endInfo ) = 0;
1527 virtual void sectionEndedEarly( SectionEndInfo const& endInfo ) = 0;
1528
1529 virtual void benchmarkStarting( BenchmarkInfo const& info ) = 0;
1530 virtual void benchmarkEnded( BenchmarkStats const& stats ) = 0;
1531
1532 virtual void pushScopedMessage( MessageInfo const& message ) = 0;
1533 virtual void popScopedMessage( MessageInfo const& message ) = 0;
1534
1535 virtual void handleFatalErrorCondition( StringRef message ) = 0;
1536
1537 virtual void handleExpr
1538 ( AssertionInfo const& info,
1539 ITransientExpression const& expr,
1540 AssertionReaction& reaction ) = 0;
1541 virtual void handleMessage
1542 ( AssertionInfo const& info,
1543 ResultWas::OfType resultType,
1544 StringRef const& message,
1545 AssertionReaction& reaction ) = 0;
1546 virtual void handleUnexpectedExceptionNotThrown
1547 ( AssertionInfo const& info,
1548 AssertionReaction& reaction ) = 0;
1549 virtual void handleUnexpectedInflightException
1550 ( AssertionInfo const& info,
1551 std::string const& message,
1552 AssertionReaction& reaction ) = 0;
1553 virtual void handleIncomplete
1554 ( AssertionInfo const& info ) = 0;
1555 virtual void handleNonExpr
1556 ( AssertionInfo const &info,
1557 ResultWas::OfType resultType,
1558 AssertionReaction &reaction ) = 0;
1559
1560 virtual bool lastAssertionPassed() = 0;
1561 virtual void assertionPassed() = 0;
1562
1563 // Deprecated, do not use:
1564 virtual std::string getCurrentTestName() const = 0;
1565 virtual const AssertionResult* getLastResult() const = 0;
1566 virtual void exceptionEarlyReported() = 0;
1567 };
1568
1569 IResultCapture& getResultCapture();
1570}
1571
1572// end catch_interfaces_capture.h
1573namespace Catch {
1574
1575 struct TestFailureException{};
1576 struct AssertionResultData;
1577 struct IResultCapture;
1578 class RunContext;
1579
1580 class LazyExpression {
1581 friend class AssertionHandler;
1582 friend struct AssertionStats;
1583 friend class RunContext;
1584
1585 ITransientExpression const* m_transientExpression = nullptr;
1586 bool m_isNegated;
1587 public:
1588 LazyExpression( bool isNegated );
1589 LazyExpression( LazyExpression const& other );
1590 LazyExpression& operator = ( LazyExpression const& ) = delete;
1591
1592 explicit operator bool() const;
1593
1594 friend auto operator << ( std::ostream& os, LazyExpression const& lazyExpr ) -> std::ostream&;
1595 };
1596
1597 struct AssertionReaction {
1598 bool shouldDebugBreak = false;
1599 bool shouldThrow = false;
1600 };
1601
1602 class AssertionHandler {
1603 AssertionInfo m_assertionInfo;
1604 AssertionReaction m_reaction;
1605 bool m_completed = false;
1606 IResultCapture& m_resultCapture;
1607
1608 public:
1609 AssertionHandler
1610 ( StringRef macroName,
1611 SourceLineInfo const& lineInfo,
1612 StringRef capturedExpression,
1613 ResultDisposition::Flags resultDisposition );
1614 ~AssertionHandler() {
1615 if ( !m_completed ) {
1616 m_resultCapture.handleIncomplete( m_assertionInfo );
1617 }
1618 }
1619
1620 template<typename T>
1621 void handleExpr( ExprLhs<T> const& expr ) {
1622 handleExpr( expr.makeUnaryExpr() );
1623 }
1624 void handleExpr( ITransientExpression const& expr );
1625
1626 void handleMessage(ResultWas::OfType resultType, StringRef const& message);
1627
1628 void handleExceptionThrownAsExpected();
1629 void handleUnexpectedExceptionNotThrown();
1630 void handleExceptionNotThrownAsExpected();
1631 void handleThrowingCallSkipped();
1632 void handleUnexpectedInflightException();
1633
1634 void complete();
1635 void setCompleted();
1636
1637 // query
1638 auto allowThrows() const -> bool;
1639 };
1640
1641 void handleExceptionMatchExpr( AssertionHandler& handler, std::string const& str, StringRef matcherString );
1642
1643} // namespace Catch
1644
1645// end catch_assertionhandler.h
1646// start catch_message.h
1647
1648#include <string>
1649
1650namespace Catch {
1651
1652 struct MessageInfo {
1653 MessageInfo( std::string const& _macroName,
1654 SourceLineInfo const& _lineInfo,
1655 ResultWas::OfType _type );
1656
1657 std::string macroName;
1658 std::string message;
1659 SourceLineInfo lineInfo;
1660 ResultWas::OfType type;
1661 unsigned int sequence;
1662
1663 bool operator == ( MessageInfo const& other ) const;
1664 bool operator < ( MessageInfo const& other ) const;
1665 private:
1666 static unsigned int globalCount;
1667 };
1668
1669 struct MessageStream {
1670
1671 template<typename T>
1672 MessageStream& operator << ( T const& value ) {
1673 m_stream << value;
1674 return *this;
1675 }
1676
1677 ReusableStringStream m_stream;
1678 };
1679
1680 struct MessageBuilder : MessageStream {
1681 MessageBuilder( std::string const& macroName,
1682 SourceLineInfo const& lineInfo,
1683 ResultWas::OfType type );
1684
1685 template<typename T>
1686 MessageBuilder& operator << ( T const& value ) {
1687 m_stream << value;
1688 return *this;
1689 }
1690
1691 MessageInfo m_info;
1692 };
1693
1694 class ScopedMessage {
1695 public:
1696 explicit ScopedMessage( MessageBuilder const& builder );
1697 ~ScopedMessage();
1698
1699 MessageInfo m_info;
1700 };
1701
1702} // end namespace Catch
1703
1704// end catch_message.h
1705#if !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE)
1706
1707#if !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_STRINGIFICATION)
1708 #define CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(...) #__VA_ARGS__
1709#else
1710 #define CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(...) "Disabled by CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_STRINGIFICATION"
1711#endif
1712
1713#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_FAST_COMPILE)
1714
1716// Another way to speed-up compilation is to omit local try-catch for REQUIRE*
1717// macros.
1718#define INTERNAL_CATCH_TRY
1719#define INTERNAL_CATCH_CATCH( capturer )
1720
1721#else // CATCH_CONFIG_FAST_COMPILE
1722
1723#define INTERNAL_CATCH_TRY try
1724#define INTERNAL_CATCH_CATCH( handler ) catch(...) { handler.handleUnexpectedInflightException(); }
1725
1726#endif
1727
1728#define INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( handler ) handler.complete();
1729
1731#define INTERNAL_CATCH_TEST( macroName, resultDisposition, ... ) \
1732 do { \
1733 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(__VA_ARGS__), resultDisposition ); \
1734 INTERNAL_CATCH_TRY { \
1735 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS \
1736 catchAssertionHandler.handleExpr( Catch::Decomposer() <= __VA_ARGS__ ); \
1737 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_PARENTHESES_WARNINGS \
1738 } INTERNAL_CATCH_CATCH( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1739 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1740 } while( (void)0, false && static_cast<bool>( !!(__VA_ARGS__) ) ) // the expression here is never evaluated at runtime but it forces the compiler to give it a look
1741 // The double negation silences MSVC's C4800 warning, the static_cast forces short-circuit evaluation if the type has overloaded &&.
1742
1744#define INTERNAL_CATCH_IF( macroName, resultDisposition, ... ) \
1745 INTERNAL_CATCH_TEST( macroName, resultDisposition, __VA_ARGS__ ); \
1746 if( Catch::getResultCapture().lastAssertionPassed() )
1747
1749#define INTERNAL_CATCH_ELSE( macroName, resultDisposition, ... ) \
1750 INTERNAL_CATCH_TEST( macroName, resultDisposition, __VA_ARGS__ ); \
1751 if( !Catch::getResultCapture().lastAssertionPassed() )
1752
1754#define INTERNAL_CATCH_NO_THROW( macroName, resultDisposition, ... ) \
1755 do { \
1756 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(__VA_ARGS__), resultDisposition ); \
1757 try { \
1758 static_cast<void>(__VA_ARGS__); \
1759 catchAssertionHandler.handleExceptionNotThrownAsExpected(); \
1760 } \
1761 catch( ... ) { \
1762 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedInflightException(); \
1763 } \
1764 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1765 } while( false )
1766
1768#define INTERNAL_CATCH_THROWS( macroName, resultDisposition, ... ) \
1769 do { \
1770 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(__VA_ARGS__), resultDisposition); \
1771 if( catchAssertionHandler.allowThrows() ) \
1772 try { \
1773 static_cast<void>(__VA_ARGS__); \
1774 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedExceptionNotThrown(); \
1775 } \
1776 catch( ... ) { \
1777 catchAssertionHandler.handleExceptionThrownAsExpected(); \
1778 } \
1779 else \
1780 catchAssertionHandler.handleThrowingCallSkipped(); \
1781 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1782 } while( false )
1783
1785#define INTERNAL_CATCH_THROWS_AS( macroName, exceptionType, resultDisposition, expr ) \
1786 do { \
1787 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(expr) ", " CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(exceptionType), resultDisposition ); \
1788 if( catchAssertionHandler.allowThrows() ) \
1789 try { \
1790 static_cast<void>(expr); \
1791 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedExceptionNotThrown(); \
1792 } \
1793 catch( exceptionType const& ) { \
1794 catchAssertionHandler.handleExceptionThrownAsExpected(); \
1795 } \
1796 catch( ... ) { \
1797 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedInflightException(); \
1798 } \
1799 else \
1800 catchAssertionHandler.handleThrowingCallSkipped(); \
1801 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1802 } while( false )
1803
1805#define INTERNAL_CATCH_MSG( macroName, messageType, resultDisposition, ... ) \
1806 do { \
1807 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, Catch::StringRef(), resultDisposition ); \
1808 catchAssertionHandler.handleMessage( messageType, ( Catch::MessageStream() << __VA_ARGS__ + ::Catch::StreamEndStop() ).m_stream.str() ); \
1809 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1810 } while( false )
1811
1813#define INTERNAL_CATCH_INFO( macroName, log ) \
1814 Catch::ScopedMessage INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( scopedMessage )( Catch::MessageBuilder( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, Catch::ResultWas::Info ) << log );
1815
1817// Although this is matcher-based, it can be used with just a string
1818#define INTERNAL_CATCH_THROWS_STR_MATCHES( macroName, resultDisposition, matcher, ... ) \
1819 do { \
1820 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(__VA_ARGS__) ", " CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(matcher), resultDisposition ); \
1821 if( catchAssertionHandler.allowThrows() ) \
1822 try { \
1823 static_cast<void>(__VA_ARGS__); \
1824 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedExceptionNotThrown(); \
1825 } \
1826 catch( ... ) { \
1827 Catch::handleExceptionMatchExpr( catchAssertionHandler, matcher, #matcher ); \
1828 } \
1829 else \
1830 catchAssertionHandler.handleThrowingCallSkipped(); \
1831 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
1832 } while( false )
1833
1834#endif // CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE
1835
1836// end catch_capture.hpp
1837// start catch_section.h
1838
1839// start catch_section_info.h
1840
1841// start catch_totals.h
1842
1843#include <cstddef>
1844
1845namespace Catch {
1846
1847 struct Counts {
1848 Counts operator - ( Counts const& other ) const;
1849 Counts& operator += ( Counts const& other );
1850
1851 std::size_t total() const;
1852 bool allPassed() const;
1853 bool allOk() const;
1854
1855 std::size_t passed = 0;
1856 std::size_t failed = 0;
1857 std::size_t failedButOk = 0;
1858 };
1859
1860 struct Totals {
1861
1862 Totals operator - ( Totals const& other ) const;
1863 Totals& operator += ( Totals const& other );
1864
1865 Totals delta( Totals const& prevTotals ) const;
1866
1867 int error = 0;
1868 Counts assertions;
1869 Counts testCases;
1870 };
1871}
1872
1873// end catch_totals.h
1874#include <string>
1875
1876namespace Catch {
1877
1878 struct SectionInfo {
1879 SectionInfo
1880 ( SourceLineInfo const& _lineInfo,
1881 std::string const& _name );
1882
1883 // Deprecated
1884 SectionInfo
1885 ( SourceLineInfo const& _lineInfo,
1886 std::string const& _name,
1887 std::string const& ) : SectionInfo( _lineInfo, _name ) {}
1888
1889 std::string name;
1890 std::string description; // !Deprecated: this will always be empty
1891 SourceLineInfo lineInfo;
1892 };
1893
1894 struct SectionEndInfo {
1895 SectionInfo sectionInfo;
1896 Counts prevAssertions;
1897 double durationInSeconds;
1898 };
1899
1900} // end namespace Catch
1901
1902// end catch_section_info.h
1903// start catch_timer.h
1904
1905#include <cstdint>
1906
1907namespace Catch {
1908
1909 auto getCurrentNanosecondsSinceEpoch() -> uint64_t;
1910 auto getEstimatedClockResolution() -> uint64_t;
1911
1912 class Timer {
1913 uint64_t m_nanoseconds = 0;
1914 public:
1915 void start();
1916 auto getElapsedNanoseconds() const -> uint64_t;
1917 auto getElapsedMicroseconds() const -> uint64_t;
1918 auto getElapsedMilliseconds() const -> unsigned int;
1919 auto getElapsedSeconds() const -> double;
1920 };
1921
1922} // namespace Catch
1923
1924// end catch_timer.h
1925#include <string>
1926
1927namespace Catch {
1928
1929 class Section : NonCopyable {
1930 public:
1931 Section( SectionInfo const& info );
1932 ~Section();
1933
1934 // This indicates whether the section should be executed or not
1935 explicit operator bool() const;
1936
1937 private:
1938 SectionInfo m_info;
1939
1940 std::string m_name;
1941 Counts m_assertions;
1942 bool m_sectionIncluded;
1943 Timer m_timer;
1944 };
1945
1946} // end namespace Catch
1947
1948#define INTERNAL_CATCH_SECTION( ... ) \
1949 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS \
1950 if( Catch::Section const& INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( catch_internal_Section ) = Catch::SectionInfo( CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, __VA_ARGS__ ) ) \
1951 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS
1952
1953#define INTERNAL_CATCH_DYNAMIC_SECTION( ... ) \
1954 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS \
1955 if( Catch::Section const& INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( catch_internal_Section ) = Catch::SectionInfo( CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, (Catch::ReusableStringStream() << __VA_ARGS__).str() ) ) \
1956 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_UNUSED_WARNINGS
1957
1958// end catch_section.h
1959// start catch_benchmark.h
1960
1961#include <cstdint>
1962#include <string>
1963
1964namespace Catch {
1965
1966 class BenchmarkLooper {
1967
1968 std::string m_name;
1969 std::size_t m_count = 0;
1970 std::size_t m_iterationsToRun = 1;
1971 uint64_t m_resolution;
1972 Timer m_timer;
1973
1974 static auto getResolution() -> uint64_t;
1975 public:
1976 // Keep most of this inline as it's on the code path that is being timed
1977 BenchmarkLooper( StringRef name )
1978 : m_name( name ),
1979 m_resolution( getResolution() )
1980 {
1981 reportStart();
1982 m_timer.start();
1983 }
1984
1985 explicit operator bool() {
1986 if( m_count < m_iterationsToRun )
1987 return true;
1988 return needsMoreIterations();
1989 }
1990
1991 void increment() {
1992 ++m_count;
1993 }
1994
1995 void reportStart();
1996 auto needsMoreIterations() -> bool;
1997 };
1998
1999} // end namespace Catch
2000
2001#define BENCHMARK( name ) \
2002 for( Catch::BenchmarkLooper looper( name ); looper; looper.increment() )
2003
2004// end catch_benchmark.h
2005// start catch_interfaces_exception.h
2006
2007// start catch_interfaces_registry_hub.h
2008
2009#include <string>
2010#include <memory>
2011
2012namespace Catch {
2013
2014 class TestCase;
2015 struct ITestCaseRegistry;
2016 struct IExceptionTranslatorRegistry;
2017 struct IExceptionTranslator;
2018 struct IReporterRegistry;
2019 struct IReporterFactory;
2020 struct ITagAliasRegistry;
2021 class StartupExceptionRegistry;
2022
2023 using IReporterFactoryPtr = std::shared_ptr<IReporterFactory>;
2024
2025 struct IRegistryHub {
2026 virtual ~IRegistryHub();
2027
2028 virtual IReporterRegistry const& getReporterRegistry() const = 0;
2029 virtual ITestCaseRegistry const& getTestCaseRegistry() const = 0;
2030 virtual ITagAliasRegistry const& getTagAliasRegistry() const = 0;
2031
2032 virtual IExceptionTranslatorRegistry& getExceptionTranslatorRegistry() = 0;
2033
2034 virtual StartupExceptionRegistry const& getStartupExceptionRegistry() const = 0;
2035 };
2036
2037 struct IMutableRegistryHub {
2038 virtual ~IMutableRegistryHub();
2039 virtual void registerReporter( std::string const& name, IReporterFactoryPtr const& factory ) = 0;
2040 virtual void registerListener( IReporterFactoryPtr const& factory ) = 0;
2041 virtual void registerTest( TestCase const& testInfo ) = 0;
2042 virtual void registerTranslator( const IExceptionTranslator* translator ) = 0;
2043 virtual void registerTagAlias( std::string const& alias, std::string const& tag, SourceLineInfo const& lineInfo ) = 0;
2044 virtual void registerStartupException() noexcept = 0;
2045 };
2046
2047 IRegistryHub& getRegistryHub();
2048 IMutableRegistryHub& getMutableRegistryHub();
2049 void cleanUp();
2050 std::string translateActiveException();
2051
2052}
2053
2054// end catch_interfaces_registry_hub.h
2055#if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE)
2056 #define INTERNAL_CATCH_TRANSLATE_EXCEPTION_NO_REG( translatorName, signature) \
2057 static std::string translatorName( signature )
2058#endif
2059
2060#include <exception>
2061#include <string>
2062#include <vector>
2063
2064namespace Catch {
2065 using exceptionTranslateFunction = std::string(*)();
2066
2067 struct IExceptionTranslator;
2068 using ExceptionTranslators = std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IExceptionTranslator const>>;
2069
2070 struct IExceptionTranslator {
2071 virtual ~IExceptionTranslator();
2072 virtual std::string translate( ExceptionTranslators::const_iterator it, ExceptionTranslators::const_iterator itEnd ) const = 0;
2073 };
2074
2075 struct IExceptionTranslatorRegistry {
2076 virtual ~IExceptionTranslatorRegistry();
2077
2078 virtual std::string translateActiveException() const = 0;
2079 };
2080
2081 class ExceptionTranslatorRegistrar {
2082 template<typename T>
2083 class ExceptionTranslator : public IExceptionTranslator {
2084 public:
2085
2086 ExceptionTranslator( std::string(*translateFunction)( T& ) )
2087 : m_translateFunction( translateFunction )
2088 {}
2089
2090 std::string translate( ExceptionTranslators::const_iterator it, ExceptionTranslators::const_iterator itEnd ) const override {
2091 try {
2092 if( it == itEnd )
2093 std::rethrow_exception(std::current_exception());
2094 else
2095 return (*it)->translate( it+1, itEnd );
2096 }
2097 catch( T& ex ) {
2098 return m_translateFunction( ex );
2099 }
2100 }
2101
2102 protected:
2103 std::string(*m_translateFunction)( T& );
2104 };
2105
2106 public:
2107 template<typename T>
2108 ExceptionTranslatorRegistrar( std::string(*translateFunction)( T& ) ) {
2109 getMutableRegistryHub().registerTranslator
2110 ( new ExceptionTranslator<T>( translateFunction ) );
2111 }
2112 };
2113}
2114
2116#define INTERNAL_CATCH_TRANSLATE_EXCEPTION2( translatorName, signature ) \
2117 static std::string translatorName( signature ); \
2118 CATCH_INTERNAL_SUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
2119 namespace{ Catch::ExceptionTranslatorRegistrar INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( catch_internal_ExceptionRegistrar )( &translatorName ); } \
2120 CATCH_INTERNAL_UNSUPPRESS_GLOBALS_WARNINGS \
2121 static std::string translatorName( signature )
2122
2123#define INTERNAL_CATCH_TRANSLATE_EXCEPTION( signature ) INTERNAL_CATCH_TRANSLATE_EXCEPTION2( INTERNAL_CATCH_UNIQUE_NAME( catch_internal_ExceptionTranslator ), signature )
2124
2125// end catch_interfaces_exception.h
2126// start catch_approx.h
2127
2128#include <type_traits>
2129#include <stdexcept>
2130
2131namespace Catch {
2132namespace Detail {
2133
2134 class Approx {
2135 private:
2136 bool equalityComparisonImpl(double other) const;
2137
2138 public:
2139 explicit Approx ( double value );
2140
2141 static Approx custom();
2142
2143 Approx operator-() const;
2144
2145 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2146 Approx operator()( T const& value ) {
2147 Approx approx( static_cast<double>(value) );
2148 approx.epsilon( m_epsilon );
2149 approx.margin( m_margin );
2150 approx.scale( m_scale );
2151 return approx;
2152 }
2153
2154 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2155 explicit Approx( T const& value ): Approx(static_cast<double>(value))
2156 {}
2157
2158 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2159 friend bool operator == ( const T& lhs, Approx const& rhs ) {
2160 auto lhs_v = static_cast<double>(lhs);
2161 return rhs.equalityComparisonImpl(lhs_v);
2162 }
2163
2164 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2165 friend bool operator == ( Approx const& lhs, const T& rhs ) {
2166 return operator==( rhs, lhs );
2167 }
2168
2169 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2170 friend bool operator != ( T const& lhs, Approx const& rhs ) {
2171 return !operator==( lhs, rhs );
2172 }
2173
2174 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2175 friend bool operator != ( Approx const& lhs, T const& rhs ) {
2176 return !operator==( rhs, lhs );
2177 }
2178
2179 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2180 friend bool operator <= ( T const& lhs, Approx const& rhs ) {
2181 return static_cast<double>(lhs) < rhs.m_value || lhs == rhs;
2182 }
2183
2184 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2185 friend bool operator <= ( Approx const& lhs, T const& rhs ) {
2186 return lhs.m_value < static_cast<double>(rhs) || lhs == rhs;
2187 }
2188
2189 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2190 friend bool operator >= ( T const& lhs, Approx const& rhs ) {
2191 return static_cast<double>(lhs) > rhs.m_value || lhs == rhs;
2192 }
2193
2194 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2195 friend bool operator >= ( Approx const& lhs, T const& rhs ) {
2196 return lhs.m_value > static_cast<double>(rhs) || lhs == rhs;
2197 }
2198
2199 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2200 Approx& epsilon( T const& newEpsilon ) {
2201 double epsilonAsDouble = static_cast<double>(newEpsilon);
2202 if( epsilonAsDouble < 0 || epsilonAsDouble > 1.0 ) {
2203 throw std::domain_error
2204 ( "Invalid Approx::epsilon: " +
2205 Catch::Detail::stringify( epsilonAsDouble ) +
2206 ", Approx::epsilon has to be between 0 and 1" );
2207 }
2208 m_epsilon = epsilonAsDouble;
2209 return *this;
2210 }
2211
2212 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2213 Approx& margin( T const& newMargin ) {
2214 double marginAsDouble = static_cast<double>(newMargin);
2215 if( marginAsDouble < 0 ) {
2216 throw std::domain_error
2217 ( "Invalid Approx::margin: " +
2218 Catch::Detail::stringify( marginAsDouble ) +
2219 ", Approx::Margin has to be non-negative." );
2220
2221 }
2222 m_margin = marginAsDouble;
2223 return *this;
2224 }
2225
2226 template <typename T, typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<double, T>::value>::type>
2227 Approx& scale( T const& newScale ) {
2228 m_scale = static_cast<double>(newScale);
2229 return *this;
2230 }
2231
2232 std::string toString() const;
2233
2234 private:
2235 double m_epsilon;
2236 double m_margin;
2237 double m_scale;
2238 double m_value;
2239 };
2240} // end namespace Detail
2241
2242namespace literals {
2243 Detail::Approx operator "" _a(long double val);
2244 Detail::Approx operator "" _a(unsigned long long val);
2245} // end namespace literals
2246
2247template<>
2248struct StringMaker<Catch::Detail::Approx> {
2249 static std::string convert(Catch::Detail::Approx const& value);
2250};
2251
2252} // end namespace Catch
2253
2254// end catch_approx.h
2255// start catch_string_manip.h
2256
2257#include <string>
2258#include <iosfwd>
2259
2260namespace Catch {
2261
2262 bool startsWith( std::string const& s, std::string const& prefix );
2263 bool startsWith( std::string const& s, char prefix );
2264 bool endsWith( std::string const& s, std::string const& suffix );
2265 bool endsWith( std::string const& s, char suffix );
2266 bool contains( std::string const& s, std::string const& infix );
2267 void toLowerInPlace( std::string& s );
2268 std::string toLower( std::string const& s );
2269 std::string trim( std::string const& str );
2270 bool replaceInPlace( std::string& str, std::string const& replaceThis, std::string const& withThis );
2271
2272 struct pluralise {
2273 pluralise( std::size_t count, std::string const& label );
2274
2275 friend std::ostream& operator << ( std::ostream& os, pluralise const& pluraliser );
2276
2277 std::size_t m_count;
2278 std::string m_label;
2279 };
2280}
2281
2282// end catch_string_manip.h
2283#ifndef CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_MATCHERS
2284// start catch_capture_matchers.h
2285
2286// start catch_matchers.h
2287
2288#include <string>
2289#include <vector>
2290
2291namespace Catch {
2292namespace Matchers {
2293 namespace Impl {
2294
2295 template<typename ArgT> struct MatchAllOf;
2296 template<typename ArgT> struct MatchAnyOf;
2297 template<typename ArgT> struct MatchNotOf;
2298
2299 class MatcherUntypedBase {
2300 public:
2301 MatcherUntypedBase() = default;
2302 MatcherUntypedBase ( MatcherUntypedBase const& ) = default;
2303 MatcherUntypedBase& operator = ( MatcherUntypedBase const& ) = delete;
2304 std::string toString() const;
2305
2306 protected:
2307 virtual ~MatcherUntypedBase();
2308 virtual std::string describe() const = 0;
2309 mutable std::string m_cachedToString;
2310 };
2311
2312 template<typename ObjectT>
2313 struct MatcherMethod {
2314 virtual bool match( ObjectT const& arg ) const = 0;
2315 };
2316 template<typename PtrT>
2317 struct MatcherMethod<PtrT*> {
2318 virtual bool match( PtrT* arg ) const = 0;
2319 };
2320
2321 template<typename T>
2322 struct MatcherBase : MatcherUntypedBase, MatcherMethod<T> {
2323
2324 MatchAllOf<T> operator && ( MatcherBase const& other ) const;
2325 MatchAnyOf<T> operator || ( MatcherBase const& other ) const;
2326 MatchNotOf<T> operator ! () const;
2327 };
2328
2329 template<typename ArgT>
2330 struct MatchAllOf : MatcherBase<ArgT> {
2331 bool match( ArgT const& arg ) const override {
2332 for( auto matcher : m_matchers ) {
2333 if (!matcher->match(arg))
2334 return false;
2335 }
2336 return true;
2337 }
2338 std::string describe() const override {
2339 std::string description;
2340 description.reserve( 4 + m_matchers.size()*32 );
2341 description += "( ";
2342 bool first = true;
2343 for( auto matcher : m_matchers ) {
2344 if( first )
2345 first = false;
2346 else
2347 description += " and ";
2348 description += matcher->toString();
2349 }
2350 description += " )";
2351 return description;
2352 }
2353
2354 MatchAllOf<ArgT>& operator && ( MatcherBase<ArgT> const& other ) {
2355 m_matchers.push_back( &other );
2356 return *this;
2357 }
2358
2359 std::vector<MatcherBase<ArgT> const*> m_matchers;
2360 };
2361 template<typename ArgT>
2362 struct MatchAnyOf : MatcherBase<ArgT> {
2363
2364 bool match( ArgT const& arg ) const override {
2365 for( auto matcher : m_matchers ) {
2366 if (matcher->match(arg))
2367 return true;
2368 }
2369 return false;
2370 }
2371 std::string describe() const override {
2372 std::string description;
2373 description.reserve( 4 + m_matchers.size()*32 );
2374 description += "( ";
2375 bool first = true;
2376 for( auto matcher : m_matchers ) {
2377 if( first )
2378 first = false;
2379 else
2380 description += " or ";
2381 description += matcher->toString();
2382 }
2383 description += " )";
2384 return description;
2385 }
2386
2387 MatchAnyOf<ArgT>& operator || ( MatcherBase<ArgT> const& other ) {
2388 m_matchers.push_back( &other );
2389 return *this;
2390 }
2391
2392 std::vector<MatcherBase<ArgT> const*> m_matchers;
2393 };
2394
2395 template<typename ArgT>
2396 struct MatchNotOf : MatcherBase<ArgT> {
2397
2398 MatchNotOf( MatcherBase<ArgT> const& underlyingMatcher ) : m_underlyingMatcher( underlyingMatcher ) {}
2399
2400 bool match( ArgT const& arg ) const override {
2401 return !m_underlyingMatcher.match( arg );
2402 }
2403
2404 std::string describe() const override {
2405 return "not " + m_underlyingMatcher.toString();
2406 }
2407 MatcherBase<ArgT> const& m_underlyingMatcher;
2408 };
2409
2410 template<typename T>
2411 MatchAllOf<T> MatcherBase<T>::operator && ( MatcherBase const& other ) const {
2412 return MatchAllOf<T>() && *this && other;
2413 }
2414 template<typename T>
2415 MatchAnyOf<T> MatcherBase<T>::operator || ( MatcherBase const& other ) const {
2416 return MatchAnyOf<T>() || *this || other;
2417 }
2418 template<typename T>
2419 MatchNotOf<T> MatcherBase<T>::operator ! () const {
2420 return MatchNotOf<T>( *this );
2421 }
2422
2423 } // namespace Impl
2424
2425} // namespace Matchers
2426
2427using namespace Matchers;
2428using Matchers::Impl::MatcherBase;
2429
2430} // namespace Catch
2431
2432// end catch_matchers.h
2433// start catch_matchers_floating.h
2434
2435#include <type_traits>
2436#include <cmath>
2437
2438namespace Catch {
2439namespace Matchers {
2440
2441 namespace Floating {
2442
2443 enum class FloatingPointKind : uint8_t;
2444
2445 struct WithinAbsMatcher : MatcherBase<double> {
2446 WithinAbsMatcher(double target, double margin);
2447 bool match(double const& matchee) const override;
2448 std::string describe() const override;
2449 private:
2450 double m_target;
2451 double m_margin;
2452 };
2453
2454 struct WithinUlpsMatcher : MatcherBase<double> {
2455 WithinUlpsMatcher(double target, int ulps, FloatingPointKind baseType);
2456 bool match(double const& matchee) const override;
2457 std::string describe() const override;
2458 private:
2459 double m_target;
2460 int m_ulps;
2461 FloatingPointKind m_type;
2462 };
2463
2464 } // namespace Floating
2465
2466 // The following functions create the actual matcher objects.
2467 // This allows the types to be inferred
2468 Floating::WithinUlpsMatcher WithinULP(double target, int maxUlpDiff);
2469 Floating::WithinUlpsMatcher WithinULP(float target, int maxUlpDiff);
2470 Floating::WithinAbsMatcher WithinAbs(double target, double margin);
2471
2472} // namespace Matchers
2473} // namespace Catch
2474
2475// end catch_matchers_floating.h
2476// start catch_matchers_generic.hpp
2477
2478#include <functional>
2479#include <string>
2480
2481namespace Catch {
2482namespace Matchers {
2483namespace Generic {
2484
2485namespace Detail {
2486 std::string finalizeDescription(const std::string& desc);
2487}
2488
2489template <typename T>
2490class PredicateMatcher : public MatcherBase<T> {
2491 std::function<bool(T const&)> m_predicate;
2492 std::string m_description;
2493public:
2494
2495 PredicateMatcher(std::function<bool(T const&)> const& elem, std::string const& descr)
2496 :m_predicate(std::move(elem)),
2497 m_description(Detail::finalizeDescription(descr))
2498 {}
2499
2500 bool match( T const& item ) const override {
2501 return m_predicate(item);
2502 }
2503
2504 std::string describe() const override {
2505 return m_description;
2506 }
2507};
2508
2509} // namespace Generic
2510
2511 // The following functions create the actual matcher objects.
2512 // The user has to explicitly specify type to the function, because
2513 // infering std::function<bool(T const&)> is hard (but possible) and
2514 // requires a lot of TMP.
2515 template<typename T>
2516 Generic::PredicateMatcher<T> Predicate(std::function<bool(T const&)> const& predicate, std::string const& description = "") {
2517 return Generic::PredicateMatcher<T>(predicate, description);
2518 }
2519
2520} // namespace Matchers
2521} // namespace Catch
2522
2523// end catch_matchers_generic.hpp
2524// start catch_matchers_string.h
2525
2526#include <string>
2527
2528namespace Catch {
2529namespace Matchers {
2530
2531 namespace StdString {
2532
2533 struct CasedString
2534 {
2535 CasedString( std::string const& str, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity );
2536 std::string adjustString( std::string const& str ) const;
2537 std::string caseSensitivitySuffix() const;
2538
2539 CaseSensitive::Choice m_caseSensitivity;
2540 std::string m_str;
2541 };
2542
2543 struct StringMatcherBase : MatcherBase<std::string> {
2544 StringMatcherBase( std::string const& operation, CasedString const& comparator );
2545 std::string describe() const override;
2546
2547 CasedString m_comparator;
2548 std::string m_operation;
2549 };
2550
2551 struct EqualsMatcher : StringMatcherBase {
2552 EqualsMatcher( CasedString const& comparator );
2553 bool match( std::string const& source ) const override;
2554 };
2555 struct ContainsMatcher : StringMatcherBase {
2556 ContainsMatcher( CasedString const& comparator );
2557 bool match( std::string const& source ) const override;
2558 };
2559 struct StartsWithMatcher : StringMatcherBase {
2560 StartsWithMatcher( CasedString const& comparator );
2561 bool match( std::string const& source ) const override;
2562 };
2563 struct EndsWithMatcher : StringMatcherBase {
2564 EndsWithMatcher( CasedString const& comparator );
2565 bool match( std::string const& source ) const override;
2566 };
2567
2568 struct RegexMatcher : MatcherBase<std::string> {
2569 RegexMatcher( std::string regex, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity );
2570 bool match( std::string const& matchee ) const override;
2571 std::string describe() const override;
2572
2573 private:
2574 std::string m_regex;
2575 CaseSensitive::Choice m_caseSensitivity;
2576 };
2577
2578 } // namespace StdString
2579
2580 // The following functions create the actual matcher objects.
2581 // This allows the types to be inferred
2582
2583 StdString::EqualsMatcher Equals( std::string const& str, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity = CaseSensitive::Yes );
2584 StdString::ContainsMatcher Contains( std::string const& str, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity = CaseSensitive::Yes );
2585 StdString::EndsWithMatcher EndsWith( std::string const& str, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity = CaseSensitive::Yes );
2586 StdString::StartsWithMatcher StartsWith( std::string const& str, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity = CaseSensitive::Yes );
2587 StdString::RegexMatcher Matches( std::string const& regex, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity = CaseSensitive::Yes );
2588
2589} // namespace Matchers
2590} // namespace Catch
2591
2592// end catch_matchers_string.h
2593// start catch_matchers_vector.h
2594
2595#include <algorithm>
2596
2597namespace Catch {
2598namespace Matchers {
2599
2600 namespace Vector {
2601 namespace Detail {
2602 template <typename InputIterator, typename T>
2603 size_t count(InputIterator first, InputIterator last, T const& item) {
2604 size_t cnt = 0;
2605 for (; first != last; ++first) {
2606 if (*first == item) {
2607 ++cnt;
2608 }
2609 }
2610 return cnt;
2611 }
2612 template <typename InputIterator, typename T>
2613 bool contains(InputIterator first, InputIterator last, T const& item) {
2614 for (; first != last; ++first) {
2615 if (*first == item) {
2616 return true;
2617 }
2618 }
2619 return false;
2620 }
2621 }
2622
2623 template<typename T>
2624 struct ContainsElementMatcher : MatcherBase<std::vector<T>> {
2625
2626 ContainsElementMatcher(T const &comparator) : m_comparator( comparator) {}
2627
2628 bool match(std::vector<T> const &v) const override {
2629 for (auto const& el : v) {
2630 if (el == m_comparator) {
2631 return true;
2632 }
2633 }
2634 return false;
2635 }
2636
2637 std::string describe() const override {
2638 return "Contains: " + ::Catch::Detail::stringify( m_comparator );
2639 }
2640
2641 T const& m_comparator;
2642 };
2643
2644 template<typename T>
2645 struct ContainsMatcher : MatcherBase<std::vector<T>> {
2646
2647 ContainsMatcher(std::vector<T> const &comparator) : m_comparator( comparator ) {}
2648
2649 bool match(std::vector<T> const &v) const override {
2650 // !TBD: see note in EqualsMatcher
2651 if (m_comparator.size() > v.size())
2652 return false;
2653 for (auto const& comparator : m_comparator) {
2654 auto present = false;
2655 for (const auto& el : v) {
2656 if (el == comparator) {
2657 present = true;
2658 break;
2659 }
2660 }
2661 if (!present) {
2662 return false;
2663 }
2664 }
2665 return true;
2666 }
2667 std::string describe() const override {
2668 return "Contains: " + ::Catch::Detail::stringify( m_comparator );
2669 }
2670
2671 std::vector<T> const& m_comparator;
2672 };
2673
2674 template<typename T>
2675 struct EqualsMatcher : MatcherBase<std::vector<T>> {
2676
2677 EqualsMatcher(std::vector<T> const &comparator) : m_comparator( comparator ) {}
2678
2679 bool match(std::vector<T> const &v) const override {
2680 // !TBD: This currently works if all elements can be compared using !=
2681 // - a more general approach would be via a compare template that defaults
2682 // to using !=. but could be specialised for, e.g. std::vector<T> etc
2683 // - then just call that directly
2684 if (m_comparator.size() != v.size())
2685 return false;
2686 for (std::size_t i = 0; i < v.size(); ++i)
2687 if (m_comparator[i] != v[i])
2688 return false;
2689 return true;
2690 }
2691 std::string describe() const override {
2692 return "Equals: " + ::Catch::Detail::stringify( m_comparator );
2693 }
2694 std::vector<T> const& m_comparator;
2695 };
2696
2697 template<typename T>
2698 struct UnorderedEqualsMatcher : MatcherBase<std::vector<T>> {
2699 UnorderedEqualsMatcher(std::vector<T> const& target) : m_target(target) {}
2700 bool match(std::vector<T> const& vec) const override {
2701 // Note: This is a reimplementation of std::is_permutation,
2702 // because I don't want to include <algorithm> inside the common path
2703 if (m_target.size() != vec.size()) {
2704 return false;
2705 }
2706 auto lfirst = m_target.begin(), llast = m_target.end();
2707 auto rfirst = vec.begin(), rlast = vec.end();
2708 // Cut common prefix to optimize checking of permuted parts
2709 while (lfirst != llast && *lfirst != *rfirst) {
2710 ++lfirst; ++rfirst;
2711 }
2712 if (lfirst == llast) {
2713 return true;
2714 }
2715
2716 for (auto mid = lfirst; mid != llast; ++mid) {
2717 // Skip already counted items
2718 if (Detail::contains(lfirst, mid, *mid)) {
2719 continue;
2720 }
2721 size_t num_vec = Detail::count(rfirst, rlast, *mid);
2722 if (num_vec == 0 || Detail::count(lfirst, llast, *mid) != num_vec) {
2723 return false;
2724 }
2725 }
2726
2727 return true;
2728 }
2729
2730 std::string describe() const override {
2731 return "UnorderedEquals: " + ::Catch::Detail::stringify(m_target);
2732 }
2733 private:
2734 std::vector<T> const& m_target;
2735 };
2736
2737 } // namespace Vector
2738
2739 // The following functions create the actual matcher objects.
2740 // This allows the types to be inferred
2741
2742 template<typename T>
2743 Vector::ContainsMatcher<T> Contains( std::vector<T> const& comparator ) {
2744 return Vector::ContainsMatcher<T>( comparator );
2745 }
2746
2747 template<typename T>
2748 Vector::ContainsElementMatcher<T> VectorContains( T const& comparator ) {
2749 return Vector::ContainsElementMatcher<T>( comparator );
2750 }
2751
2752 template<typename T>
2753 Vector::EqualsMatcher<T> Equals( std::vector<T> const& comparator ) {
2754 return Vector::EqualsMatcher<T>( comparator );
2755 }
2756
2757 template<typename T>
2758 Vector::UnorderedEqualsMatcher<T> UnorderedEquals(std::vector<T> const& target) {
2759 return Vector::UnorderedEqualsMatcher<T>(target);
2760 }
2761
2762} // namespace Matchers
2763} // namespace Catch
2764
2765// end catch_matchers_vector.h
2766namespace Catch {
2767
2768 template<typename ArgT, typename MatcherT>
2769 class MatchExpr : public ITransientExpression {
2770 ArgT const& m_arg;
2771 MatcherT m_matcher;
2772 StringRef m_matcherString;
2773 public:
2774 MatchExpr( ArgT const& arg, MatcherT const& matcher, StringRef matcherString )
2775 : ITransientExpression{ true, matcher.match( arg ) },
2776 m_arg( arg ),
2777 m_matcher( matcher ),
2778 m_matcherString( matcherString )
2779 {}
2780
2781 void streamReconstructedExpression( std::ostream &os ) const override {
2782 auto matcherAsString = m_matcher.toString();
2783 os << Catch::Detail::stringify( m_arg ) << ' ';
2784 if( matcherAsString == Detail::unprintableString )
2785 os << m_matcherString;
2786 else
2787 os << matcherAsString;
2788 }
2789 };
2790
2791 using StringMatcher = Matchers::Impl::MatcherBase<std::string>;
2792
2793 void handleExceptionMatchExpr( AssertionHandler& handler, StringMatcher const& matcher, StringRef matcherString );
2794
2795 template<typename ArgT, typename MatcherT>
2796 auto makeMatchExpr( ArgT const& arg, MatcherT const& matcher, StringRef matcherString ) -> MatchExpr<ArgT, MatcherT> {
2797 return MatchExpr<ArgT, MatcherT>( arg, matcher, matcherString );
2798 }
2799
2800} // namespace Catch
2801
2803#define INTERNAL_CHECK_THAT( macroName, matcher, resultDisposition, arg ) \
2804 do { \
2805 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(arg) ", " CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(matcher), resultDisposition ); \
2806 INTERNAL_CATCH_TRY { \
2807 catchAssertionHandler.handleExpr( Catch::makeMatchExpr( arg, matcher, #matcher ) ); \
2808 } INTERNAL_CATCH_CATCH( catchAssertionHandler ) \
2809 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
2810 } while( false )
2811
2813#define INTERNAL_CATCH_THROWS_MATCHES( macroName, exceptionType, resultDisposition, matcher, ... ) \
2814 do { \
2815 Catch::AssertionHandler catchAssertionHandler( macroName, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO, CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(__VA_ARGS__) ", " CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(exceptionType) ", " CATCH_INTERNAL_STRINGIFY(matcher), resultDisposition ); \
2816 if( catchAssertionHandler.allowThrows() ) \
2817 try { \
2818 static_cast<void>(__VA_ARGS__ ); \
2819 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedExceptionNotThrown(); \
2820 } \
2821 catch( exceptionType const& ex ) { \
2822 catchAssertionHandler.handleExpr( Catch::makeMatchExpr( ex, matcher, #matcher ) ); \
2823 } \
2824 catch( ... ) { \
2825 catchAssertionHandler.handleUnexpectedInflightException(); \
2826 } \
2827 else \
2828 catchAssertionHandler.handleThrowingCallSkipped(); \
2829 INTERNAL_CATCH_REACT( catchAssertionHandler ) \
2830 } while( false )
2831
2832// end catch_capture_matchers.h
2833#endif
2834
2835// These files are included here so the single_include script doesn't put them
2836// in the conditionally compiled sections
2837// start catch_test_case_info.h
2838
2839#include <string>
2840#include <vector>
2841#include <memory>
2842
2843#ifdef __clang__
2844#pragma clang diagnostic push
2845#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wpadded"
2846#endif
2847
2848namespace Catch {
2849
2850 struct ITestInvoker;
2851
2852 struct TestCaseInfo {
2853 enum SpecialProperties{
2854 None = 0,
2855 IsHidden = 1 << 1,
2856 ShouldFail = 1 << 2,
2857 MayFail = 1 << 3,
2858 Throws = 1 << 4,
2859 NonPortable = 1 << 5,
2860 Benchmark = 1 << 6
2861 };
2862
2863 TestCaseInfo( std::string const& _name,
2864 std::string const& _className,
2865 std::string const& _description,
2866 std::vector<std::string> const& _tags,
2867 SourceLineInfo const& _lineInfo );
2868
2869 friend void setTags( TestCaseInfo& testCaseInfo, std::vector<std::string> tags );
2870
2871 bool isHidden() const;
2872 bool throws() const;
2873 bool okToFail() const;
2874 bool expectedToFail() const;
2875
2876 std::string tagsAsString() const;
2877
2878 std::string name;
2879 std::string className;
2880 std::string description;
2881 std::vector<std::string> tags;
2882 std::vector<std::string> lcaseTags;
2883 SourceLineInfo lineInfo;
2884 SpecialProperties properties;
2885 };
2886
2887 class TestCase : public TestCaseInfo {
2888 public:
2889
2890 TestCase( ITestInvoker* testCase, TestCaseInfo&& info );
2891
2892 TestCase withName( std::string const& _newName ) const;
2893
2894 void invoke() const;
2895
2896 TestCaseInfo const& getTestCaseInfo() const;
2897
2898 bool operator == ( TestCase const& other ) const;
2899 bool operator < ( TestCase const& other ) const;
2900
2901 private:
2902 std::shared_ptr<ITestInvoker> test;
2903 };
2904
2905 TestCase makeTestCase( ITestInvoker* testCase,
2906 std::string const& className,
2907 NameAndTags const& nameAndTags,
2908 SourceLineInfo const& lineInfo );
2909}
2910
2911#ifdef __clang__
2912#pragma clang diagnostic pop
2913#endif
2914
2915// end catch_test_case_info.h
2916// start catch_interfaces_runner.h
2917
2918namespace Catch {
2919
2920 struct IRunner {
2921 virtual ~IRunner();
2922 virtual bool aborting() const = 0;
2923 };
2924}

2926// end catch_interfaces_runner.h
2927
2928#ifdef __OBJC__
2929// start catch_objc.hpp
2930
2931#import <objc/runtime.h>
2932
2933#include <string>
2934
2935// NB. Any general catch headers included here must be included
2936// in catch.hpp first to make sure they are included by the single
2937// header for non obj-usage
2938
2940// This protocol is really only here for (self) documenting purposes, since
2941// all its methods are optional.
2942@protocol OcFixture
2943
2944@optional
2945
2946-(void) setUp;
2947-(void) tearDown;
2948
2949@end
2950
2951namespace Catch {
2952
2953 class OcMethod : public ITestInvoker {
2954
2955 public:
2956 OcMethod( Class cls, SEL sel ) : m_cls( cls ), m_sel( sel ) {}
2957
2958 virtual void invoke() const {
2959 id obj = [[m_cls alloc] init];
2960
2961 performOptionalSelector( obj, @selector(setUp) );
2962 performOptionalSelector( obj, m_sel );
2963 performOptionalSelector( obj, @selector(tearDown) );
2964
2965 arcSafeRelease( obj );
2966 }
2967 private:
2968 virtual ~OcMethod() {}
2969
2970 Class m_cls;
2971 SEL m_sel;
2972 };
2973
2974 namespace Detail{
2975
2976 inline std::string getAnnotation( Class cls,
2977 std::string const& annotationName,
2978 std::string const& testCaseName ) {
2979 NSString* selStr = [[NSString alloc] initWithFormat:@"Catch_%s_%s", annotationName.c_str(), testCaseName.c_str()];
2980 SEL sel = NSSelectorFromString( selStr );
2981 arcSafeRelease( selStr );
2982 id value = performOptionalSelector( cls, sel );
2983 if( value )
2984 return [(NSString*)value UTF8String];
2985 return "";
2986 }
2987 }
2988
2989 inline std::size_t registerTestMethods() {
2990 std::size_t noTestMethods = 0;
2991 int noClasses = objc_getClassList( nullptr, 0 );
2992
2993 Class* classes = (CATCH_UNSAFE_UNRETAINED Class *)malloc( sizeof(Class) * noClasses);
2994 objc_getClassList( classes, noClasses );
2995
2996 for( int c = 0; c < noClasses; c++ ) {
2997 Class cls = classes[c];
2998 {
2999 u_int count;
3000 Method* methods = class_copyMethodList( cls, &count );
3001 for( u_int m = 0; m < count ; m++ ) {
3002 SEL selector = method_getName(methods[m]);
3003 std::string methodName = sel_getName(selector);
3004 if( startsWith( methodName, "Catch_TestCase_" ) ) {
3005 std::string testCaseName = methodName.substr( 15 );
3006 std::string name = Detail::getAnnotation( cls, "Name", testCaseName );
3007 std::string desc = Detail::getAnnotation( cls, "Description", testCaseName );
3008 const char* className = class_getName( cls );
3009
3010 getMutableRegistryHub().registerTest( makeTestCase( new OcMethod( cls, selector ), className, NameAndTags( name.c_str(), desc.c_str() ), SourceLineInfo("",0) ) );
3011 noTestMethods++;
3012 }
3013 }
3014 free(methods);
3015 }
3016 }
3017 return noTestMethods;
3018 }
3019
3020#if !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_MATCHERS)
3021
3022 namespace Matchers {
3023 namespace Impl {
3024 namespace NSStringMatchers {
3025
3026 struct StringHolder : MatcherBase<NSString*>{
3027 StringHolder( NSString* substr ) : m_substr( [substr copy] ){}
3028 StringHolder( StringHolder const& other ) : m_substr( [other.m_substr copy] ){}
3029 StringHolder() {
3030 arcSafeRelease( m_substr );
3031 }
3032
3033 bool match( NSString* arg ) const override {
3034 return false;
3035 }
3036
3037 NSString* CATCH_ARC_STRONG m_substr;
3038 };
3039
3040 struct Equals : StringHolder {
3041 Equals( NSString* substr ) : StringHolder( substr ){}
3042
3043 bool match( NSString* str ) const override {
3044 return (str != nil || m_substr == nil ) &&
3045 [str isEqualToString:m_substr];
3046 }
3047
3048 std::string describe() const override {
3049 return "equals string: " + Catch::Detail::stringify( m_substr );
3050 }
3051 };
3052
3053 struct Contains : StringHolder {
3054 Contains( NSString* substr ) : StringHolder( substr ){}
3055
3056 bool match( NSString* str ) const {
3057 return (str != nil || m_substr == nil ) &&
3058 [str rangeOfString:m_substr].location != NSNotFound;
3059 }
3060
3061 std::string describe() const override {
3062 return "contains string: " + Catch::Detail::stringify( m_substr );
3063 }
3064 };
3065
3066 struct StartsWith : StringHolder {
3067 StartsWith( NSString* substr ) : StringHolder( substr ){}
3068
3069 bool match( NSString* str ) const override {
3070 return (str != nil || m_substr == nil ) &&
3071 [str rangeOfString:m_substr].location == 0;
3072 }
3073
3074 std::string describe() const override {
3075 return "starts with: " + Catch::Detail::stringify( m_substr );
3076 }
3077 };
3078 struct EndsWith : StringHolder {
3079 EndsWith( NSString* substr ) : StringHolder( substr ){}
3080
3081 bool match( NSString* str ) const override {
3082 return (str != nil || m_substr == nil ) &&
3083 [str rangeOfString:m_substr].location == [str length] - [m_substr length];
3084 }
3085
3086 std::string describe() const override {
3087 return "ends with: " + Catch::Detail::stringify( m_substr );
3088 }
3089 };
3090
3091 } // namespace NSStringMatchers
3092 } // namespace Impl
3093
3094 inline Impl::NSStringMatchers::Equals
3095 Equals( NSString* substr ){ return Impl::NSStringMatchers::Equals( substr ); }
3096
3097 inline Impl::NSStringMatchers::Contains
3098 Contains( NSString* substr ){ return Impl::NSStringMatchers::Contains( substr ); }
3099
3100 inline Impl::NSStringMatchers::StartsWith
3101 StartsWith( NSString* substr ){ return Impl::NSStringMatchers::StartsWith( substr ); }
3102
3103 inline Impl::NSStringMatchers::EndsWith
3104 EndsWith( NSString* substr ){ return Impl::NSStringMatchers::EndsWith( substr ); }
3105
3106 } // namespace Matchers
3107
3108 using namespace Matchers;
3109
3110#endif // CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_MATCHERS
3111
3112} // namespace Catch
3113
3115#define OC_MAKE_UNIQUE_NAME( root, uniqueSuffix ) root##uniqueSuffix
3116#define OC_TEST_CASE2( name, desc, uniqueSuffix ) \
3117+(NSString*) OC_MAKE_UNIQUE_NAME( Catch_Name_test_, uniqueSuffix ) \
3118{ \
3119return @ name; \
3120} \
3121+(NSString*) OC_MAKE_UNIQUE_NAME( Catch_Description_test_, uniqueSuffix ) \
3122{ \
3123return @ desc; \
3124} \
3125-(void) OC_MAKE_UNIQUE_NAME( Catch_TestCase_test_, uniqueSuffix )
3126
3127#define OC_TEST_CASE( name, desc ) OC_TEST_CASE2( name, desc, __LINE__ )
3128
3129// end catch_objc.hpp
3130#endif

3155// start catch_wildcard_pattern.h
3156
3157namespace Catch
3158{
3159 class WildcardPattern {
3160 enum WildcardPosition {
3161 NoWildcard = 0,
3162 WildcardAtStart = 1,
3163 WildcardAtEnd = 2,
3164 WildcardAtBothEnds = WildcardAtStart | WildcardAtEnd
3165 };
3166
3167 public:
3168
3169 WildcardPattern( std::string const& pattern, CaseSensitive::Choice caseSensitivity );
3170 virtual ~WildcardPattern() = default;
3171 virtual bool matches( std::string const& str ) const;
3172
3173 private:
3174 std::string adjustCase( std::string const& str ) const;
3175 CaseSensitive::Choice m_caseSensitivity;
3176 WildcardPosition m_wildcard = NoWildcard;
3177 std::string m_pattern;
3178 };
3179}

3181// end catch_wildcard_pattern.h
3182#include <string>
3183#include <vector>
3184#include <memory>
3185
3186namespace Catch {
3187
3188 class TestSpec {
3189 struct Pattern {
3190 virtual ~Pattern();
3191 virtual bool matches( TestCaseInfo const& testCase ) const = 0;
3192 };
3193 using PatternPtr = std::shared_ptr<Pattern>;
3194
3195 class NamePattern : public Pattern {
3196 public:
3197 NamePattern( std::string const& name );
3198 virtual ~NamePattern();
3199 virtual bool matches( TestCaseInfo const& testCase ) const override;
3200 private:
3201 WildcardPattern m_wildcardPattern;
3202 };
3203
3204 class TagPattern : public Pattern {
3205 public:
3206 TagPattern( std::string const& tag );
3207 virtual ~TagPattern();
3208 virtual bool matches( TestCaseInfo const& testCase ) const override;
3209 private:
3210 std::string m_tag;
3211 };
3212
3213 class ExcludedPattern : public Pattern {
3214 public:
3215 ExcludedPattern( PatternPtr const& underlyingPattern );
3216 virtual ~ExcludedPattern();
3217 virtual bool matches( TestCaseInfo const& testCase ) const override;
3218 private:
3219 PatternPtr m_underlyingPattern;
3220 };
3221
3222 struct Filter {
3223 std::vector<PatternPtr> m_patterns;
3224
3225 bool matches( TestCaseInfo const& testCase ) const;
3226 };
3227
3228 public:
3229 bool hasFilters() const;
3230 bool matches( TestCaseInfo const& testCase ) const;
3231
3232 private:
3233 std::vector<Filter> m_filters;
3234
3235 friend class TestSpecParser;
3236 };
3237}
