5358#ifndef CATCH_CLARA_TEXTFLOW_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH
5359#define CATCH_CLARA_TEXTFLOW_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH 80
5360#endif
5361
5362namespace Catch { namespace clara { namespace TextFlow {
5363
5364 inline auto isWhitespace( char c ) -> bool {
5365 static std::string chars = " \t\n\r";
5366 return chars.find( c ) != std::string::npos;
5367 }
5368 inline auto isBreakableBefore( char c ) -> bool {
5369 static std::string chars = "[({<|";
5370 return chars.find( c ) != std::string::npos;
5371 }
5372 inline auto isBreakableAfter( char c ) -> bool {
5373 static std::string chars = "])}>.,:;*+-=&/\\";
5374 return chars.find( c ) != std::string::npos;
5375 }
5376
5377 class Columns;
5378
5379 class Column {
5380 std::vector<std::string> m_strings;
5381 size_t m_width = CATCH_CLARA_TEXTFLOW_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH;
5382 size_t m_indent = 0;
5383 size_t m_initialIndent = std::string::npos;
5384
5385 public:
5386 class iterator {
5387 friend Column;
5388
5389 Column const& m_column;
5390 size_t m_stringIndex = 0;
5391 size_t m_pos = 0;
5392
5393 size_t m_len = 0;
5394 size_t m_end = 0;
5395 bool m_suffix = false;
5396
5397 iterator( Column const& column, size_t stringIndex )
5398 : m_column( column ),
5399 m_stringIndex( stringIndex )
5400 {}
5401
5402 auto line() const -> std::string const& { return m_column.m_strings[m_stringIndex]; }
5403
5404 auto isBoundary( size_t at ) const -> bool {
5405 assert( at > 0 );
5406 assert( at <= line().size() );
5407
5408 return at == line().size() ||
5409 ( isWhitespace( line()[at] ) && !isWhitespace( line()[at-1] ) ) ||
5410 isBreakableBefore( line()[at] ) ||
5411 isBreakableAfter( line()[at-1] );
5412 }
5413
5414 void calcLength() {
5415 assert( m_stringIndex < m_column.m_strings.size() );
5416
5417 m_suffix = false;
5418 auto width = m_column.m_width-indent();
5419 m_end = m_pos;
5420 while( m_end < line().size() && line()[m_end] != '\n' )
5421 ++m_end;
5422
5423 if( m_end < m_pos + width ) {
5424 m_len = m_end - m_pos;
5425 }
5426 else {
5427 size_t len = width;
5428 while (len > 0 && !isBoundary(m_pos + len))
5429 --len;
5430 while (len > 0 && isWhitespace( line()[m_pos + len - 1] ))
5431 --len;
5432
5433 if (len > 0) {
5434 m_len = len;
5435 } else {
5436 m_suffix = true;
5437 m_len = width - 1;
5438 }
5439 }
5440 }
5441
5442 auto indent() const -> size_t {
5443 auto initial = m_pos == 0 && m_stringIndex == 0 ? m_column.m_initialIndent : std::string::npos;
5444 return initial == std::string::npos ? m_column.m_indent : initial;
5445 }
5446
5447 auto addIndentAndSuffix(std::string const &plain) const -> std::string {
5448 return std::string( indent(), ' ' ) + (m_suffix ? plain + "-" : plain);
5449 }
5450
5451 public:
5452 explicit iterator( Column const& column ) : m_column( column ) {
5453 assert( m_column.m_width > m_column.m_indent );
5454 assert( m_column.m_initialIndent == std::string::npos || m_column.m_width > m_column.m_initialIndent );
5455 calcLength();
5456 if( m_len == 0 )
5457 m_stringIndex++; // Empty string
5458 }
5459
5460 auto operator *() const -> std::string {
5461 assert( m_stringIndex < m_column.m_strings.size() );
5462 assert( m_pos <= m_end );
5463 if( m_pos + m_column.m_width < m_end )
5464 return addIndentAndSuffix(line().substr(m_pos, m_len));
5465 else
5466 return addIndentAndSuffix(line().substr(m_pos, m_end - m_pos));
5467 }
5468
5469 auto operator ++() -> iterator& {
5470 m_pos += m_len;
5471 if( m_pos < line().size() && line()[m_pos] == '\n' )
5472 m_pos += 1;
5473 else
5474 while( m_pos < line().size() && isWhitespace( line()[m_pos] ) )
5475 ++m_pos;
5476
5477 if( m_pos == line().size() ) {
5478 m_pos = 0;
5479 ++m_stringIndex;
5480 }
5481 if( m_stringIndex < m_column.m_strings.size() )
5482 calcLength();
5483 return *this;
5484 }
5485 auto operator ++(int) -> iterator {
5486 iterator prev( *this );
5487 operator++();
5488 return prev;
5489 }
5490
5491 auto operator ==( iterator const& other ) const -> bool {
5492 return
5493 m_pos == other.m_pos &&
5494 m_stringIndex == other.m_stringIndex &&
5495 &m_column == &other.m_column;
5496 }
5497 auto operator !=( iterator const& other ) const -> bool {
5498 return !operator==( other );
5499 }
5500 };
5501 using const_iterator = iterator;
5502
5503 explicit Column( std::string const& text ) { m_strings.push_back( text ); }
5504
5505 auto width( size_t newWidth ) -> Column& {
5506 assert( newWidth > 0 );
5507 m_width = newWidth;
5508 return *this;
5509 }
5510 auto indent( size_t newIndent ) -> Column& {
5511 m_indent = newIndent;
5512 return *this;
5513 }
5514 auto initialIndent( size_t newIndent ) -> Column& {
5515 m_initialIndent = newIndent;
5516 return *this;
5517 }
5518
5519 auto width() const -> size_t { return m_width; }
5520 auto begin() const -> iterator { return iterator( *this ); }
5521 auto end() const -> iterator { return { *this, m_strings.size() }; }
5522
5523 inline friend std::ostream& operator << ( std::ostream& os, Column const& col ) {
5524 bool first = true;
5525 for( auto line : col ) {
5526 if( first )
5527 first = false;
5528 else
5529 os << "\n";
5530 os << line;
5531 }
5532 return os;
5533 }
5534
5535 auto operator + ( Column const& other ) -> Columns;
5536
5537 auto toString() const -> std::string {
5538 std::ostringstream oss;
5539 oss << *this;
5540 return oss.str();
5541 }
5542 };
5543
5544 class Spacer : public Column {
5545
5546 public:
5547 explicit Spacer( size_t spaceWidth ) : Column( "" ) {
5548 width( spaceWidth );
5549 }
5550 };
5551
5552 class Columns {
5553 std::vector<Column> m_columns;
5554
5555 public:
5556
5557 class iterator {
5558 friend Columns;
5559 struct EndTag {};
5560
5561 std::vector<Column> const& m_columns;
5562 std::vector<Column::iterator> m_iterators;
5563 size_t m_activeIterators;
5564
5565 iterator( Columns const& columns, EndTag )
5566 : m_columns( columns.m_columns ),
5567 m_activeIterators( 0 )
5568 {
5569 m_iterators.reserve( m_columns.size() );
5570
5571 for( auto const& col : m_columns )
5572 m_iterators.push_back( col.end() );
5573 }
5574
5575 public:
5576 explicit iterator( Columns const& columns )
5577 : m_columns( columns.m_columns ),
5578 m_activeIterators( m_columns.size() )
5579 {
5580 m_iterators.reserve( m_columns.size() );
5581
5582 for( auto const& col : m_columns )
5583 m_iterators.push_back( col.begin() );
5584 }
5585
5586 auto operator ==( iterator const& other ) const -> bool {
5587 return m_iterators == other.m_iterators;
5588 }
5589 auto operator !=( iterator const& other ) const -> bool {
5590 return m_iterators != other.m_iterators;
5591 }
5592 auto operator *() const -> std::string {
5593 std::string row, padding;
5594
5595 for( size_t i = 0; i < m_columns.size(); ++i ) {
5596 auto width = m_columns[i].width();
5597 if( m_iterators[i] != m_columns[i].end() ) {
5598 std::string col = *m_iterators[i];
5599 row += padding + col;
5600 if( col.size() < width )
5601 padding = std::string( width - col.size(), ' ' );
5602 else
5603 padding = "";
5604 }
5605 else {
5606 padding += std::string( width, ' ' );
5607 }
5608 }
5609 return row;
5610 }
5611 auto operator ++() -> iterator& {
5612 for( size_t i = 0; i < m_columns.size(); ++i ) {
5613 if (m_iterators[i] != m_columns[i].end())
5614 ++m_iterators[i];
5615 }
5616 return *this;
5617 }
5618 auto operator ++(int) -> iterator {
5619 iterator prev( *this );
5620 operator++();
5621 return prev;
5622 }
5623 };
5624 using const_iterator = iterator;
5625
5626 auto begin() const -> iterator { return iterator( *this ); }
5627 auto end() const -> iterator { return { *this, iterator::EndTag() }; }
5628
5629 auto operator += ( Column const& col ) -> Columns& {
5630 m_columns.push_back( col );
5631 return *this;
5632 }
5633 auto operator + ( Column const& col ) -> Columns {
5634 Columns combined = *this;
5635 combined += col;
5636 return combined;
5637 }
5638
5639 inline friend std::ostream& operator << ( std::ostream& os, Columns const& cols ) {
5640
5641 bool first = true;
5642 for( auto line : cols ) {
5643 if( first )
5644 first = false;
5645 else
5646 os << "\n";
5647 os << line;
5648 }
5649 return os;
5650 }
5651
5652 auto toString() const -> std::string {
5653 std::ostringstream oss;
5654 oss << *this;
5655 return oss.str();
5656 }
5657 };
5658
5659 inline auto Column::operator + ( Column const& other ) -> Columns {
5660 Columns cols;
5661 cols += *this;
5662 cols += other;
5663 return cols;
5664 }
5665}}} // namespace Catch::clara::TextFlow
5666
5667// ----------- end of #include from clara_textflow.hpp -----------
5668// ........... back in clara.hpp
5669
5670#include <memory>
5671#include <set>
5672#include <algorithm>
5673
5674#if !defined(CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS) && ( defined(WIN32) || defined(__WIN32__) || defined(_WIN32) || defined(_MSC_VER) )
5675#define CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
5676#endif
5677
5678namespace Catch { namespace clara {
5679namespace detail {
5680
5681 // Traits for extracting arg and return type of lambdas (for single argument lambdas)
5682 template<typename L>
5683 struct UnaryLambdaTraits : UnaryLambdaTraits<decltype( &L::operator() )> {};
5684
5685 template<typename ClassT, typename ReturnT, typename... Args>
5686 struct UnaryLambdaTraits<ReturnT( ClassT::* )( Args... ) const> {
5687 static const bool isValid = false;
5688 };
5689
5690 template<typename ClassT, typename ReturnT, typename ArgT>
5691 struct UnaryLambdaTraits<ReturnT( ClassT::* )( ArgT ) const> {
5692 static const bool isValid = true;
5693 using ArgType = typename std::remove_const<typename std::remove_reference<ArgT>::type>::type;
5694 using ReturnType = ReturnT;
5695 };
5696
5697 class TokenStream;
5698
5699 // Transport for raw args (copied from main args, or supplied via init list for testing)
5700 class Args {
5701 friend TokenStream;
5702 std::string m_exeName;
5703 std::vector<std::string> m_args;
5704
5705 public:
5706 Args( int argc, char const* const* argv )
5707 : m_exeName(argv[0]),
5708 m_args(argv + 1, argv + argc) {}
5709
5710 Args( std::initializer_list<std::string> args )
5711 : m_exeName( *args.begin() ),
5712 m_args( args.begin()+1, args.end() )
5713 {}
5714
5715 auto exeName() const -> std::string {
5716 return m_exeName;
5717 }
5718 };
5719
5720 // Wraps a token coming from a token stream. These may not directly correspond to strings as a single string
5721 // may encode an option + its argument if the : or = form is used
5722 enum class TokenType {
5723 Option, Argument
5724 };
5725 struct Token {
5726 TokenType type;
5727 std::string token;
5728 };
5729
5730 inline auto isOptPrefix( char c ) -> bool {
5731 return c == '-'
5732#ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
5733 || c == '/'
5734#endif
5735 ;
5736 }
5737
5738 // Abstracts iterators into args as a stream of tokens, with option arguments uniformly handled
5739 class TokenStream {
5740 using Iterator = std::vector<std::string>::const_iterator;
5741 Iterator it;
5742 Iterator itEnd;
5743 std::vector<Token> m_tokenBuffer;
5744
5745 void loadBuffer() {
5746 m_tokenBuffer.resize( 0 );
5747
5748 // Skip any empty strings
5749 while( it != itEnd && it->empty() )
5750 ++it;
5751
5752 if( it != itEnd ) {
5753 auto const &next = *it;
5754 if( isOptPrefix( next[0] ) ) {
5755 auto delimiterPos = next.find_first_of( " :=" );
5756 if( delimiterPos != std::string::npos ) {
5757 m_tokenBuffer.push_back( { TokenType::Option, next.substr( 0, delimiterPos ) } );
5758 m_tokenBuffer.push_back( { TokenType::Argument, next.substr( delimiterPos + 1 ) } );
5759 } else {
5760 if( next[1] != '-' && next.size() > 2 ) {
5761 std::string opt = "- ";
5762 for( size_t i = 1; i < next.size(); ++i ) {
5763 opt[1] = next[i];
5764 m_tokenBuffer.push_back( { TokenType::Option, opt } );
5765 }
5766 } else {
5767 m_tokenBuffer.push_back( { TokenType::Option, next } );
5768 }
5769 }
5770 } else {
5771 m_tokenBuffer.push_back( { TokenType::Argument, next } );
5772 }
5773 }
5774 }
5775
5776 public:
5777 explicit TokenStream( Args const &args ) : TokenStream( args.m_args.begin(), args.m_args.end() ) {}
5778
5779 TokenStream( Iterator it, Iterator itEnd ) : it( it ), itEnd( itEnd ) {
5780 loadBuffer();
5781 }
5782
5783 explicit operator bool() const {
5784 return !m_tokenBuffer.empty() || it != itEnd;
5785 }
5786
5787 auto count() const -> size_t { return m_tokenBuffer.size() + (itEnd - it); }
5788
5789 auto operator*() const -> Token {
5790 assert( !m_tokenBuffer.empty() );
5791 return m_tokenBuffer.front();
5792 }
5793
5794 auto operator->() const -> Token const * {
5795 assert( !m_tokenBuffer.empty() );
5796 return &m_tokenBuffer.front();
5797 }
5798
5799 auto operator++() -> TokenStream & {
5800 if( m_tokenBuffer.size() >= 2 ) {
5801 m_tokenBuffer.erase( m_tokenBuffer.begin() );
5802 } else {
5803 if( it != itEnd )
5804 ++it;
5805 loadBuffer();
5806 }
5807 return *this;
5808 }
5809 };
5810
5811 class ResultBase {
5812 public:
5813 enum Type {
5814 Ok, LogicError, RuntimeError
5815 };
5816
5817 protected:
5818 ResultBase( Type type ) : m_type( type ) {}
5819 virtual ~ResultBase() = default;
5820
5821 virtual void enforceOk() const = 0;
5822
5823 Type m_type;
5824 };
5825
5826 template<typename T>
5827 class ResultValueBase : public ResultBase {
5828 public:
5829 auto value() const -> T const & {
5830 enforceOk();
5831 return m_value;
5832 }
5833
5834 protected:
5835 ResultValueBase( Type type ) : ResultBase( type ) {}
5836
5837 ResultValueBase( ResultValueBase const &other ) : ResultBase( other ) {
5838 if( m_type == ResultBase::Ok )
5839 new( &m_value ) T( other.m_value );
5840 }
5841
5842 ResultValueBase( Type, T const &value ) : ResultBase( Ok ) {
5843 new( &m_value ) T( value );
5844 }
5845
5846 auto operator=( ResultValueBase const &other ) -> ResultValueBase & {
5847 if( m_type == ResultBase::Ok )
5848 m_value.~T();
5849 ResultBase::operator=(other);
5850 if( m_type == ResultBase::Ok )
5851 new( &m_value ) T( other.m_value );
5852 return *this;
5853 }
5854
5855 ~ResultValueBase() override {
5856 if( m_type == Ok )
5857 m_value.~T();
5858 }
5859
5860 union {
5861 T m_value;
5862 };
5863 };
5864
5865 template<>
5866 class ResultValueBase<void> : public ResultBase {
5867 protected:
5868 using ResultBase::ResultBase;
5869 };
5870
5871 template<typename T = void>
5872 class BasicResult : public ResultValueBase<T> {
5873 public:
5874 template<typename U>
5875 explicit BasicResult( BasicResult<U> const &other )
5876 : ResultValueBase<T>( other.type() ),
5877 m_errorMessage( other.errorMessage() )
5878 {
5879 assert( type() != ResultBase::Ok );
5880 }
5881
5882 template<typename U>
5883 static auto ok( U const &value ) -> BasicResult { return { ResultBase::Ok, value }; }
5884 static auto ok() -> BasicResult { return { ResultBase::Ok }; }
5885 static auto logicError( std::string const &message ) -> BasicResult { return { ResultBase::LogicError, message }; }
5886 static auto runtimeError( std::string const &message ) -> BasicResult { return { ResultBase::RuntimeError, message }; }
5887
5888 explicit operator bool() const { return m_type == ResultBase::Ok; }
5889 auto type() const -> ResultBase::Type { return m_type; }
5890 auto errorMessage() const -> std::string { return m_errorMessage; }
5891
5892 protected:
5893 void enforceOk() const override {
5894
5895 // Errors shouldn't reach this point, but if they do
5896 // the actual error message will be in m_errorMessage
5897 assert( m_type != ResultBase::LogicError );
5898 assert( m_type != ResultBase::RuntimeError );
5899 if( m_type != ResultBase::Ok )
5900 std::abort();
5901 }
5902
5903 std::string m_errorMessage; // Only populated if resultType is an error
5904
5905 BasicResult( ResultBase::Type type, std::string const &message )
5906 : ResultValueBase<T>(type),
5907 m_errorMessage(message)
5908 {
5909 assert( m_type != ResultBase::Ok );
5910 }
5911
5912 using ResultValueBase<T>::ResultValueBase;
5913 using ResultBase::m_type;
5914 };
5915
5916 enum class ParseResultType {
5917 Matched, NoMatch, ShortCircuitAll, ShortCircuitSame
5918 };
5919
5920 class ParseState {
5921 public:
5922
5923 ParseState( ParseResultType type, TokenStream const &remainingTokens )
5924 : m_type(type),
5925 m_remainingTokens( remainingTokens )
5926 {}
5927
5928 auto type() const -> ParseResultType { return m_type; }
5929 auto remainingTokens() const -> TokenStream { return m_remainingTokens; }
5930
5931 private:
5932 ParseResultType m_type;
5933 TokenStream m_remainingTokens;
5934 };
5935
5936 using Result = BasicResult<void>;
5937 using ParserResult = BasicResult<ParseResultType>;
5938 using InternalParseResult = BasicResult<ParseState>;
5939
5940 struct HelpColumns {
5941 std::string left;
5942 std::string right;
5943 };
5944
5945 template<typename T>
5946 inline auto convertInto( std::string const &source, T& target ) -> ParserResult {
5947 std::stringstream ss;
5948 ss << source;
5949 ss >> target;
5950 if( ss.fail() )
5951 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "Unable to convert '" + source + "' to destination type" );
5952 else
5953 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
5954 }
5955 inline auto convertInto( std::string const &source, std::string& target ) -> ParserResult {
5956 target = source;
5957 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
5958 }
5959 inline auto convertInto( std::string const &source, bool &target ) -> ParserResult {
5960 std::string srcLC = source;
5961 std::transform( srcLC.begin(), srcLC.end(), srcLC.begin(), []( char c ) { return static_cast<char>( ::tolower(c) ); } );
5962 if (srcLC == "y" || srcLC == "1" || srcLC == "true" || srcLC == "yes" || srcLC == "on")
5963 target = true;
5964 else if (srcLC == "n" || srcLC == "0" || srcLC == "false" || srcLC == "no" || srcLC == "off")
5965 target = false;
5966 else
5967 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "Expected a boolean value but did not recognise: '" + source + "'" );
5968 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
5969 }
5970#ifdef CLARA_CONFIG_OPTIONAL_TYPE
5971 template<typename T>
5972 inline auto convertInto( std::string const &source, CLARA_CONFIG_OPTIONAL_TYPE<T>& target ) -> ParserResult {
5973 T temp;
5974 auto result = convertInto( source, temp );
5975 if( result )
5976 target = std::move(temp);
5977 return result;
5978 }
5979#endif // CLARA_CONFIG_OPTIONAL_TYPE
5980
5981 struct NonCopyable {
5982 NonCopyable() = default;
5983 NonCopyable( NonCopyable const & ) = delete;
5984 NonCopyable( NonCopyable && ) = delete;
5985 NonCopyable &operator=( NonCopyable const & ) = delete;
5986 NonCopyable &operator=( NonCopyable && ) = delete;
5987 };
5988
5989 struct BoundRef : NonCopyable {
5990 virtual ~BoundRef() = default;
5991 virtual auto isContainer() const -> bool { return false; }
5992 virtual auto isFlag() const -> bool { return false; }
5993 };
5994 struct BoundValueRefBase : BoundRef {
5995 virtual auto setValue( std::string const &arg ) -> ParserResult = 0;
5996 };
5997 struct BoundFlagRefBase : BoundRef {
5998 virtual auto setFlag( bool flag ) -> ParserResult = 0;
5999 virtual auto isFlag() const -> bool { return true; }
6000 };
6001
6002 template<typename T>
6003 struct BoundValueRef : BoundValueRefBase {
6004 T &m_ref;
6005
6006 explicit BoundValueRef( T &ref ) : m_ref( ref ) {}
6007
6008 auto setValue( std::string const &arg ) -> ParserResult override {
6009 return convertInto( arg, m_ref );
6010 }
6011 };
6012
6013 template<typename T>
6014 struct BoundValueRef<std::vector<T>> : BoundValueRefBase {
6015 std::vector<T> &m_ref;
6016
6017 explicit BoundValueRef( std::vector<T> &ref ) : m_ref( ref ) {}
6018
6019 auto isContainer() const -> bool override { return true; }
6020
6021 auto setValue( std::string const &arg ) -> ParserResult override {
6022 T temp;
6023 auto result = convertInto( arg, temp );
6024 if( result )
6025 m_ref.push_back( temp );
6026 return result;
6027 }
6028 };
6029
6030 struct BoundFlagRef : BoundFlagRefBase {
6031 bool &m_ref;
6032
6033 explicit BoundFlagRef( bool &ref ) : m_ref( ref ) {}
6034
6035 auto setFlag( bool flag ) -> ParserResult override {
6036 m_ref = flag;
6037 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6038 }
6039 };
6040
6041 template<typename ReturnType>
6042 struct LambdaInvoker {
6043 static_assert( std::is_same<ReturnType, ParserResult>::value, "Lambda must return void or clara::ParserResult" );
6044
6045 template<typename L, typename ArgType>
6046 static auto invoke( L const &lambda, ArgType const &arg ) -> ParserResult {
6047 return lambda( arg );
6048 }
6049 };
6050
6051 template<>
6052 struct LambdaInvoker<void> {
6053 template<typename L, typename ArgType>
6054 static auto invoke( L const &lambda, ArgType const &arg ) -> ParserResult {
6055 lambda( arg );
6056 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6057 }
6058 };
6059
6060 template<typename ArgType, typename L>
6061 inline auto invokeLambda( L const &lambda, std::string const &arg ) -> ParserResult {
6062 ArgType temp{};
6063 auto result = convertInto( arg, temp );
6064 return !result
6065 ? result
6066 : LambdaInvoker<typename UnaryLambdaTraits<L>::ReturnType>::invoke( lambda, temp );
6067 }
6068
6069 template<typename L>
6070 struct BoundLambda : BoundValueRefBase {
6071 L m_lambda;
6072
6073 static_assert( UnaryLambdaTraits<L>::isValid, "Supplied lambda must take exactly one argument" );
6074 explicit BoundLambda( L const &lambda ) : m_lambda( lambda ) {}
6075
6076 auto setValue( std::string const &arg ) -> ParserResult override {
6077 return invokeLambda<typename UnaryLambdaTraits<L>::ArgType>( m_lambda, arg );
6078 }
6079 };
6080
6081 template<typename L>
6082 struct BoundFlagLambda : BoundFlagRefBase {
6083 L m_lambda;
6084
6085 static_assert( UnaryLambdaTraits<L>::isValid, "Supplied lambda must take exactly one argument" );
6086 static_assert( std::is_same<typename UnaryLambdaTraits<L>::ArgType, bool>::value, "flags must be boolean" );
6087
6088 explicit BoundFlagLambda( L const &lambda ) : m_lambda( lambda ) {}
6089
6090 auto setFlag( bool flag ) -> ParserResult override {
6091 return LambdaInvoker<typename UnaryLambdaTraits<L>::ReturnType>::invoke( m_lambda, flag );
6092 }
6093 };
6094
6095 enum class Optionality { Optional, Required };
6096
6097 struct Parser;
6098
6099 class ParserBase {
6100 public:
6101 virtual ~ParserBase() = default;
6102 virtual auto validate() const -> Result { return Result::ok(); }
6103 virtual auto parse( std::string const& exeName, TokenStream const &tokens) const -> InternalParseResult = 0;
6104 virtual auto cardinality() const -> size_t { return 1; }
6105
6106 auto parse( Args const &args ) const -> InternalParseResult {
6107 return parse( args.exeName(), TokenStream( args ) );
6108 }
6109 };
6110
6111 template<typename DerivedT>
6112 class ComposableParserImpl : public ParserBase {
6113 public:
6114 template<typename T>
6115 auto operator|( T const &other ) const -> Parser;
6116
6117 template<typename T>
6118 auto operator+( T const &other ) const -> Parser;
6119 };
6120
6121 // Common code and state for Args and Opts
6122 template<typename DerivedT>
6123 class ParserRefImpl : public ComposableParserImpl<DerivedT> {
6124 protected:
6125 Optionality m_optionality = Optionality::Optional;
6126 std::shared_ptr<BoundRef> m_ref;
6127 std::string m_hint;
6128 std::string m_description;
6129
6130 explicit ParserRefImpl( std::shared_ptr<BoundRef> const &ref ) : m_ref( ref ) {}
6131
6132 public:
6133 template<typename T>
6134 ParserRefImpl( T &ref, std::string const &hint )
6135 : m_ref( std::make_shared<BoundValueRef<T>>( ref ) ),
6136 m_hint( hint )
6137 {}
6138
6139 template<typename LambdaT>
6140 ParserRefImpl( LambdaT const &ref, std::string const &hint )
6141 : m_ref( std::make_shared<BoundLambda<LambdaT>>( ref ) ),
6142 m_hint(hint)
6143 {}
6144
6145 auto operator()( std::string const &description ) -> DerivedT & {
6146 m_description = description;
6147 return static_cast<DerivedT &>( *this );
6148 }
6149
6150 auto optional() -> DerivedT & {
6151 m_optionality = Optionality::Optional;
6152 return static_cast<DerivedT &>( *this );
6153 };
6154
6155 auto required() -> DerivedT & {
6156 m_optionality = Optionality::Required;
6157 return static_cast<DerivedT &>( *this );
6158 };
6159
6160 auto isOptional() const -> bool {
6161 return m_optionality == Optionality::Optional;
6162 }
6163
6164 auto cardinality() const -> size_t override {
6165 if( m_ref->isContainer() )
6166 return 0;
6167 else
6168 return 1;
6169 }
6170
6171 auto hint() const -> std::string { return m_hint; }
6172 };
6173
6174 class ExeName : public ComposableParserImpl<ExeName> {
6175 std::shared_ptr<std::string> m_name;
6176 std::shared_ptr<BoundValueRefBase> m_ref;
6177
6178 template<typename LambdaT>
6179 static auto makeRef(LambdaT const &lambda) -> std::shared_ptr<BoundValueRefBase> {
6180 return std::make_shared<BoundLambda<LambdaT>>( lambda) ;
6181 }
6182
6183 public:
6184 ExeName() : m_name( std::make_shared<std::string>( "<executable>" ) ) {}
6185
6186 explicit ExeName( std::string &ref ) : ExeName() {
6187 m_ref = std::make_shared<BoundValueRef<std::string>>( ref );
6188 }
6189
6190 template<typename LambdaT>
6191 explicit ExeName( LambdaT const& lambda ) : ExeName() {
6192 m_ref = std::make_shared<BoundLambda<LambdaT>>( lambda );
6193 }
6194
6195 // The exe name is not parsed out of the normal tokens, but is handled specially
6196 auto parse( std::string const&, TokenStream const &tokens ) const -> InternalParseResult override {
6197 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( ParseResultType::NoMatch, tokens ) );
6198 }
6199
6200 auto name() const -> std::string { return *m_name; }
6201 auto set( std::string const& newName ) -> ParserResult {
6202
6203 auto lastSlash = newName.find_last_of( "\\/" );
6204 auto filename = ( lastSlash == std::string::npos )
6205 ? newName
6206 : newName.substr( lastSlash+1 );
6207
6208 *m_name = filename;
6209 if( m_ref )
6210 return m_ref->setValue( filename );
6211 else
6212 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6213 }
6214 };
6215
6216 class Arg : public ParserRefImpl<Arg> {
6217 public:
6218 using ParserRefImpl::ParserRefImpl;
6219
6220 auto parse( std::string const &, TokenStream const &tokens ) const -> InternalParseResult override {
6221 auto validationResult = validate();
6222 if( !validationResult )
6223 return InternalParseResult( validationResult );
6224
6225 auto remainingTokens = tokens;
6226 auto const &token = *remainingTokens;
6227 if( token.type != TokenType::Argument )
6228 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( ParseResultType::NoMatch, remainingTokens ) );
6229
6230 assert( !m_ref->isFlag() );
6231 auto valueRef = static_cast<detail::BoundValueRefBase*>( m_ref.get() );
6232
6233 auto result = valueRef->setValue( remainingTokens->token );
6234 if( !result )
6235 return InternalParseResult( result );
6236 else
6237 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( ParseResultType::Matched, ++remainingTokens ) );
6238 }
6239 };
6240
6241 inline auto normaliseOpt( std::string const &optName ) -> std::string {
6242#ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
6243 if( optName[0] == '/' )
6244 return "-" + optName.substr( 1 );
6245 else
6246#endif
6247 return optName;
6248 }
6249
6250 class Opt : public ParserRefImpl<Opt> {
6251 protected:
6252 std::vector<std::string> m_optNames;
6253
6254 public:
6255 template<typename LambdaT>
6256 explicit Opt( LambdaT const &ref ) : ParserRefImpl( std::make_shared<BoundFlagLambda<LambdaT>>( ref ) ) {}
6257
6258 explicit Opt( bool &ref ) : ParserRefImpl( std::make_shared<BoundFlagRef>( ref ) ) {}
6259
6260 template<typename LambdaT>
6261 Opt( LambdaT const &ref, std::string const &hint ) : ParserRefImpl( ref, hint ) {}
6262
6263 template<typename T>
6264 Opt( T &ref, std::string const &hint ) : ParserRefImpl( ref, hint ) {}
6265
6266 auto operator[]( std::string const &optName ) -> Opt & {
6267 m_optNames.push_back( optName );
6268 return *this;
6269 }
6270
6271 auto getHelpColumns() const -> std::vector<HelpColumns> {
6272 std::ostringstream oss;
6273 bool first = true;
6274 for( auto const &opt : m_optNames ) {
6275 if (first)
6276 first = false;
6277 else
6278 oss << ", ";
6279 oss << opt;
6280 }
6281 if( !m_hint.empty() )
6282 oss << " <" << m_hint << ">";
6283 return { { oss.str(), m_description } };
6284 }
6285
6286 auto isMatch( std::string const &optToken ) const -> bool {
6287 auto normalisedToken = normaliseOpt( optToken );
6288 for( auto const &name : m_optNames ) {
6289 if( normaliseOpt( name ) == normalisedToken )
6290 return true;
6291 }
6292 return false;
6293 }
6294
6295 using ParserBase::parse;
6296
6297 auto parse( std::string const&, TokenStream const &tokens ) const -> InternalParseResult override {
6298 auto validationResult = validate();
6299 if( !validationResult )
6300 return InternalParseResult( validationResult );
6301
6302 auto remainingTokens = tokens;
6303 if( remainingTokens && remainingTokens->type == TokenType::Option ) {
6304 auto const &token = *remainingTokens;
6305 if( isMatch(token.token ) ) {
6306 if( m_ref->isFlag() ) {
6307 auto flagRef = static_cast<detail::BoundFlagRefBase*>( m_ref.get() );
6308 auto result = flagRef->setFlag( true );
6309 if( !result )
6310 return InternalParseResult( result );
6311 if( result.value() == ParseResultType::ShortCircuitAll )
6312 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( result.value(), remainingTokens ) );
6313 } else {
6314 auto valueRef = static_cast<detail::BoundValueRefBase*>( m_ref.get() );
6315 ++remainingTokens;
6316 if( !remainingTokens )
6317 return InternalParseResult::runtimeError( "Expected argument following " + token.token );
6318 auto const &argToken = *remainingTokens;
6319 if( argToken.type != TokenType::Argument )
6320 return InternalParseResult::runtimeError( "Expected argument following " + token.token );
6321 auto result = valueRef->setValue( argToken.token );
6322 if( !result )
6323 return InternalParseResult( result );
6324 if( result.value() == ParseResultType::ShortCircuitAll )
6325 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( result.value(), remainingTokens ) );
6326 }
6327 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( ParseResultType::Matched, ++remainingTokens ) );
6328 }
6329 }
6330 return InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( ParseResultType::NoMatch, remainingTokens ) );
6331 }
6332
6333 auto validate() const -> Result override {
6334 if( m_optNames.empty() )
6335 return Result::logicError( "No options supplied to Opt" );
6336 for( auto const &name : m_optNames ) {
6337 if( name.empty() )
6338 return Result::logicError( "Option name cannot be empty" );
6339#ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
6340 if( name[0] != '-' && name[0] != '/' )
6341 return Result::logicError( "Option name must begin with '-' or '/'" );
6342#else
6343 if( name[0] != '-' )
6344 return Result::logicError( "Option name must begin with '-'" );
6345#endif
6346 }
6347 return ParserRefImpl::validate();
6348 }
6349 };
6350
6351 struct Help : Opt {
6352 Help( bool &showHelpFlag )
6353 : Opt([&]( bool flag ) {
6354 showHelpFlag = flag;
6355 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::ShortCircuitAll );
6356 })
6357 {
6358 static_cast<Opt &>( *this )
6359 ("display usage information")
6360 ["-?"]["-h"]["--help"]
6361 .optional();
6362 }
6363 };
6364
6365 struct Parser : ParserBase {
6366
6367 mutable ExeName m_exeName;
6368 std::vector<Opt> m_options;
6369 std::vector<Arg> m_args;
6370
6371 auto operator|=( ExeName const &exeName ) -> Parser & {
6372 m_exeName = exeName;
6373 return *this;
6374 }
6375
6376 auto operator|=( Arg const &arg ) -> Parser & {
6377 m_args.push_back(arg);
6378 return *this;
6379 }
6380
6381 auto operator|=( Opt const &opt ) -> Parser & {
6382 m_options.push_back(opt);
6383 return *this;
6384 }
6385
6386 auto operator|=( Parser const &other ) -> Parser & {
6387 m_options.insert(m_options.end(), other.m_options.begin(), other.m_options.end());
6388 m_args.insert(m_args.end(), other.m_args.begin(), other.m_args.end());
6389 return *this;
6390 }
6391
6392 template<typename T>
6393 auto operator|( T const &other ) const -> Parser {
6394 return Parser( *this ) |= other;
6395 }
6396
6397 // Forward deprecated interface with '+' instead of '|'
6398 template<typename T>
6399 auto operator+=( T const &other ) -> Parser & { return operator|=( other ); }
6400 template<typename T>
6401 auto operator+( T const &other ) const -> Parser { return operator|( other ); }
6402
6403 auto getHelpColumns() const -> std::vector<HelpColumns> {
6404 std::vector<HelpColumns> cols;
6405 for (auto const &o : m_options) {
6406 auto childCols = o.getHelpColumns();
6407 cols.insert( cols.end(), childCols.begin(), childCols.end() );
6408 }
6409 return cols;
6410 }
6411
6412 void writeToStream( std::ostream &os ) const {
6413 if (!m_exeName.name().empty()) {
6414 os << "usage:\n" << " " << m_exeName.name() << " ";
6415 bool required = true, first = true;
6416 for( auto const &arg : m_args ) {
6417 if (first)
6418 first = false;
6419 else
6420 os << " ";
6421 if( arg.isOptional() && required ) {
6422 os << "[";
6423 required = false;
6424 }
6425 os << "<" << arg.hint() << ">";
6426 if( arg.cardinality() == 0 )
6427 os << " ... ";
6428 }
6429 if( !required )
6430 os << "]";
6431 if( !m_options.empty() )
6432 os << " options";
6433 os << "\n\nwhere options are:" << std::endl;
6434 }
6435
6436 auto rows = getHelpColumns();
6437 size_t consoleWidth = CATCH_CLARA_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH;
6438 size_t optWidth = 0;
6439 for( auto const &cols : rows )
6440 optWidth = (std::max)(optWidth, cols.left.size() + 2);
6441
6442 optWidth = (std::min)(optWidth, consoleWidth/2);
6443
6444 for( auto const &cols : rows ) {
6445 auto row =
6446 TextFlow::Column( cols.left ).width( optWidth ).indent( 2 ) +
6447 TextFlow::Spacer(4) +
6448 TextFlow::Column( cols.right ).width( consoleWidth - 7 - optWidth );
6449 os << row << std::endl;
6450 }
6451 }
6452
6453 friend auto operator<<( std::ostream &os, Parser const &parser ) -> std::ostream& {
6454 parser.writeToStream( os );
6455 return os;
6456 }
6457
6458 auto validate() const -> Result override {
6459 for( auto const &opt : m_options ) {
6460 auto result = opt.validate();
6461 if( !result )
6462 return result;
6463 }
6464 for( auto const &arg : m_args ) {
6465 auto result = arg.validate();
6466 if( !result )
6467 return result;
6468 }
6469 return Result::ok();
6470 }
6471
6472 using ParserBase::parse;
6473
6474 auto parse( std::string const& exeName, TokenStream const &tokens ) const -> InternalParseResult override {
6475
6476 struct ParserInfo {
6477 ParserBase const* parser = nullptr;
6478 size_t count = 0;
6479 };
6480 const size_t totalParsers = m_options.size() + m_args.size();
6481 assert( totalParsers < 512 );
6482 // ParserInfo parseInfos[totalParsers]; // <-- this is what we really want to do
6483 ParserInfo parseInfos[512];
6484
6485 {
6486 size_t i = 0;
6487 for (auto const &opt : m_options) parseInfos[i++].parser = &opt;
6488 for (auto const &arg : m_args) parseInfos[i++].parser = &arg;
6489 }
6490
6491 m_exeName.set( exeName );
6492
6493 auto result = InternalParseResult::ok( ParseState( ParseResultType::NoMatch, tokens ) );
6494 while( result.value().remainingTokens() ) {
6495 bool tokenParsed = false;
6496
6497 for( size_t i = 0; i < totalParsers; ++i ) {
6498 auto& parseInfo = parseInfos[i];
6499 if( parseInfo.parser->cardinality() == 0 || parseInfo.count < parseInfo.parser->cardinality() ) {
6500 result = parseInfo.parser->parse(exeName, result.value().remainingTokens());
6501 if (!result)
6502 return result;
6503 if (result.value().type() != ParseResultType::NoMatch) {
6504 tokenParsed = true;
6505 ++parseInfo.count;
6506 break;
6507 }
6508 }
6509 }
6510
6511 if( result.value().type() == ParseResultType::ShortCircuitAll )
6512 return result;
6513 if( !tokenParsed )
6514 return InternalParseResult::runtimeError( "Unrecognised token: " + result.value().remainingTokens()->token );
6515 }
6516 // !TBD Check missing required options
6517 return result;
6518 }
6519 };
6520
6521 template<typename DerivedT>
6522 template<typename T>
6523 auto ComposableParserImpl<DerivedT>::operator|( T const &other ) const -> Parser {
6524 return Parser() | static_cast<DerivedT const &>( *this ) | other;
6525 }
6526} // namespace detail
6527
6528// A Combined parser
6529using detail::Parser;
6530
6531// A parser for options
6532using detail::Opt;
6533
6534// A parser for arguments
6535using detail::Arg;
6536
6537// Wrapper for argc, argv from main()
6538using detail::Args;
6539
6540// Specifies the name of the executable
6541using detail::ExeName;
6542
6543// Convenience wrapper for option parser that specifies the help option
6544using detail::Help;
6545
6546// enum of result types from a parse
6547using detail::ParseResultType;
6548
6549// Result type for parser operation
6550using detail::ParserResult;
6551
6552}} // namespace Catch::clara
6553
6554// end clara.hpp
6555#ifdef __clang__
6556#pragma clang diagnostic pop
6557#endif
6558
6559// Restore Clara's value for console width, if present
6560#ifdef CATCH_TEMP_CLARA_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH
6561#define CATCH_CLARA_TEXTFLOW_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH CATCH_TEMP_CLARA_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH
6562#undef CATCH_TEMP_CLARA_CONFIG_CONSOLE_WIDTH
6563#endif
6564
6565// end catch_clara.h
6566namespace Catch {
6567
6568 clara::Parser makeCommandLineParser( ConfigData& config );
6569
6570} // end namespace Catch
6571
6572// end catch_commandline.h
6573#include <fstream>
6574#include <ctime>
6575
6576namespace Catch {
6577
6578 clara::Parser makeCommandLineParser( ConfigData& config ) {
6579
6580 using namespace clara;
6581
6582 auto const setWarning = [&]( std::string const& warning ) {
6583 auto warningSet = [&]() {
6584 if( warning == "NoAssertions" )
6585 return WarnAbout::NoAssertions;
6586
6587 if ( warning == "NoTests" )
6588 return WarnAbout::NoTests;
6589
6590 return WarnAbout::Nothing;
6591 }();
6592
6593 if (warningSet == WarnAbout::Nothing)
6594 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "Unrecognised warning: '" + warning + "'" );
6595 config.warnings = static_cast<WarnAbout::What>( config.warnings | warningSet );
6596 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6597 };
6598 auto const loadTestNamesFromFile = [&]( std::string const& filename ) {
6599 std::ifstream f( filename.c_str() );
6600 if( !f.is_open() )
6601 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "Unable to load input file: '" + filename + "'" );
6602
6603 std::string line;
6604 while( std::getline( f, line ) ) {
6605 line = trim(line);
6606 if( !line.empty() && !startsWith( line, '#' ) ) {
6607 if( !startsWith( line, '"' ) )
6608 line = '"' + line + '"';
6609 config.testsOrTags.push_back( line + ',' );
6610 }
6611 }
6612 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6613 };
6614 auto const setTestOrder = [&]( std::string const& order ) {
6615 if( startsWith( "declared", order ) )
6616 config.runOrder = RunTests::InDeclarationOrder;
6617 else if( startsWith( "lexical", order ) )
6618 config.runOrder = RunTests::InLexicographicalOrder;
6619 else if( startsWith( "random", order ) )
6620 config.runOrder = RunTests::InRandomOrder;
6621 else
6622 return clara::ParserResult::runtimeError( "Unrecognised ordering: '" + order + "'" );
6623 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6624 };
6625 auto const setRngSeed = [&]( std::string const& seed ) {
6626 if( seed != "time" )
6627 return clara::detail::convertInto( seed, config.rngSeed );
6628 config.rngSeed = static_cast<unsigned int>( std::time(nullptr) );
6629 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6630 };
6631 auto const setColourUsage = [&]( std::string const& useColour ) {
6632 auto mode = toLower( useColour );
6633
6634 if( mode == "yes" )
6635 config.useColour = UseColour::Yes;
6636 else if( mode == "no" )
6637 config.useColour = UseColour::No;
6638 else if( mode == "auto" )
6639 config.useColour = UseColour::Auto;
6640 else
6641 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "colour mode must be one of: auto, yes or no. '" + useColour + "' not recognised" );
6642 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6643 };
6644 auto const setWaitForKeypress = [&]( std::string const& keypress ) {
6645 auto keypressLc = toLower( keypress );
6646 if( keypressLc == "start" )
6647 config.waitForKeypress = WaitForKeypress::BeforeStart;
6648 else if( keypressLc == "exit" )
6649 config.waitForKeypress = WaitForKeypress::BeforeExit;
6650 else if( keypressLc == "both" )
6651 config.waitForKeypress = WaitForKeypress::BeforeStartAndExit;
6652 else
6653 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "keypress argument must be one of: start, exit or both. '" + keypress + "' not recognised" );
6654 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6655 };
6656 auto const setVerbosity = [&]( std::string const& verbosity ) {
6657 auto lcVerbosity = toLower( verbosity );
6658 if( lcVerbosity == "quiet" )
6659 config.verbosity = Verbosity::Quiet;
6660 else if( lcVerbosity == "normal" )
6661 config.verbosity = Verbosity::Normal;
6662 else if( lcVerbosity == "high" )
6663 config.verbosity = Verbosity::High;
6664 else
6665 return ParserResult::runtimeError( "Unrecognised verbosity, '" + verbosity + "'" );
6666 return ParserResult::ok( ParseResultType::Matched );
6667 };
6668
6669 auto cli
6670 = ExeName( config.processName )
6671 | Help( config.showHelp )
6672 | Opt( config.listTests )
6673 ["-l"]["--list-tests"]
6674 ( "list all/matching test cases" )
6675 | Opt( config.listTags )
6676 ["-t"]["--list-tags"]
6677 ( "list all/matching tags" )
6678 | Opt( config.showSuccessfulTests )
6679 ["-s"]["--success"]
6680 ( "include successful tests in output" )
6681 | Opt( config.shouldDebugBreak )
6682 ["-b"]["--break"]
6683 ( "break into debugger on failure" )
6684 | Opt( config.noThrow )
6685 ["-e"]["--nothrow"]
6686 ( "skip exception tests" )
6687 | Opt( config.showInvisibles )
6688 ["-i"]["--invisibles"]
6689 ( "show invisibles (tabs, newlines)" )
6690 | Opt( config.outputFilename, "filename" )
6691 ["-o"]["--out"]
6692 ( "output filename" )
6693 | Opt( config.reporterName, "name" )
6694 ["-r"]["--reporter"]
6695 ( "reporter to use (defaults to console)" )
6696 | Opt( config.name, "name" )
6697 ["-n"]["--name"]
6698 ( "suite name" )
6699 | Opt( [&]( bool ){ config.abortAfter = 1; } )
6700 ["-a"]["--abort"]
6701 ( "abort at first failure" )
6702 | Opt( [&]( int x ){ config.abortAfter = x; }, "no. failures" )
6703 ["-x"]["--abortx"]
6704 ( "abort after x failures" )
6705 | Opt( setWarning, "warning name" )
6706 ["-w"]["--warn"]
6707 ( "enable warnings" )
6708 | Opt( [&]( bool flag ) { config.showDurations = flag ? ShowDurations::Always : ShowDurations::Never; }, "yes|no" )
6709 ["-d"]["--durations"]
6710 ( "show test durations" )
6711 | Opt( loadTestNamesFromFile, "filename" )
6712 ["-f"]["--input-file"]
6713 ( "load test names to run from a file" )
6714 | Opt( config.filenamesAsTags )
6715 ["-#"]["--filenames-as-tags"]
6716 ( "adds a tag for the filename" )
6717 | Opt( config.sectionsToRun, "section name" )
6718 ["-c"]["--section"]
6719 ( "specify section to run" )
6720 | Opt( setVerbosity, "quiet|normal|high" )
6721 ["-v"]["--verbosity"]
6722 ( "set output verbosity" )
6723 | Opt( config.listTestNamesOnly )
6724 ["--list-test-names-only"]
6725 ( "list all/matching test cases names only" )
6726 | Opt( config.listReporters )
6727 ["--list-reporters"]
6728 ( "list all reporters" )
6729 | Opt( setTestOrder, "decl|lex|rand" )
6730 ["--order"]
6731 ( "test case order (defaults to decl)" )
6732 | Opt( setRngSeed, "'time'|number" )
6733 ["--rng-seed"]
6734 ( "set a specific seed for random numbers" )
6735 | Opt( setColourUsage, "yes|no" )
6736 ["--use-colour"]
6737 ( "should output be colourised" )
6738 | Opt( config.libIdentify )
6739 ["--libidentify"]
6740 ( "report name and version according to libidentify standard" )
6741 | Opt( setWaitForKeypress, "start|exit|both" )
6742 ["--wait-for-keypress"]
6743 ( "waits for a keypress before exiting" )
6744 | Opt( config.benchmarkResolutionMultiple, "multiplier" )
6745 ["--benchmark-resolution-multiple"]
6746 ( "multiple of clock resolution to run benchmarks" )
6747
6748 | Arg( config.testsOrTags, "test name|pattern|tags" )
6749 ( "which test or tests to use" );
6750
6751 return cli;
6752 }
6753
6754} // end namespace Catch
6755// end catch_commandline.cpp
6756// start catch_common.cpp
6757
6758#include <cstring>
6759#include <ostream>
6760
6761namespace Catch {
6762
6763 bool SourceLineInfo::empty() const noexcept {
6764 return file[0] == '\0';
6765 }
6766 bool SourceLineInfo::operator == ( SourceLineInfo const& other ) const noexcept {
6767 return line == other.line && (file == other.file || std::strcmp(file, other.file) == 0);
6768 }
6769 bool SourceLineInfo::operator < ( SourceLineInfo const& other ) const noexcept {
6770 return line < other.line || ( line == other.line && (std::strcmp(file, other.file) < 0));
6771 }
6772
6773 std::ostream& operator << ( std::ostream& os, SourceLineInfo const& info ) {
6774#ifndef __GNUG__
6775 os << info.file << '(' << info.line << ')';
6776#else
6777 os << info.file << ':' << info.line;
6778#endif
6779 return os;
6780 }
6781
6782 std::string StreamEndStop::operator+() const {
6783 return std::string();
6784 }
6785
6786 NonCopyable::NonCopyable() = default;
6787 NonCopyable::~NonCopyable() = default;
6788
6789}
6790// end catch_common.cpp
6791// start catch_config.cpp
6792
6793// start catch_enforce.h
6794
6795#include <stdexcept>
6796
6797#define CATCH_PREPARE_EXCEPTION( type, msg ) \
6798 type( ( Catch::ReusableStringStream() << msg ).str() )
6799#define CATCH_INTERNAL_ERROR( msg ) \
6800 throw CATCH_PREPARE_EXCEPTION( std::logic_error, CATCH_INTERNAL_LINEINFO << ": Internal Catch error: " << msg);
6801#define CATCH_ERROR( msg ) \
6802 throw CATCH_PREPARE_EXCEPTION( std::domain_error, msg )
6803#define CATCH_ENFORCE( condition, msg ) \
6804 do{ if( !(condition) ) CATCH_ERROR( msg ); } while(false)
6805
6806// end catch_enforce.h
6807namespace Catch {
6808
6809 Config::Config( ConfigData const& data )
6810 : m_data( data ),
6811 m_stream( openStream() )
6812 {
6813 TestSpecParser parser(ITagAliasRegistry::get());
6814 if (data.testsOrTags.empty()) {
6815 parser.parse("~[.]"); // All not hidden tests
6816 }
6817 else {
6818 m_hasTestFilters = true;
6819 for( auto const& testOrTags : data.testsOrTags )
6820 parser.parse( testOrTags );
6821 }
6822 m_testSpec = parser.testSpec();
6823 }
6824
6825 std::string const& Config::getFilename() const {
6826 return m_data.outputFilename ;
6827 }
6828
6829 bool Config::listTests() const { return m_data.listTests; }
6830 bool Config::listTestNamesOnly() const { return m_data.listTestNamesOnly; }
6831 bool Config::listTags() const { return m_data.listTags; }
6832 bool Config::listReporters() const { return m_data.listReporters; }
6833
6834 std::string Config::getProcessName() const { return m_data.processName; }
6835 std::string const& Config::getReporterName() const { return m_data.reporterName; }
6836
6837 std::vector<std::string> const& Config::getTestsOrTags() const { return m_data.testsOrTags; }
6838 std::vector<std::string> const& Config::getSectionsToRun() const { return m_data.sectionsToRun; }
6839
6840 TestSpec const& Config::testSpec() const { return m_testSpec; }
6841 bool Config::hasTestFilters() const { return m_hasTestFilters; }
6842
6843 bool Config::showHelp() const { return m_data.showHelp; }
6844
6845 // IConfig interface
6846 bool Config::allowThrows() const { return !m_data.noThrow; }
6847 std::ostream& Config::stream() const { return m_stream->stream(); }
6848 std::string Config::name() const { return m_data.name.empty() ? m_data.processName : m_data.name; }
6849 bool Config::includeSuccessfulResults() const { return m_data.showSuccessfulTests; }
6850 bool Config::warnAboutMissingAssertions() const { return !!(m_data.warnings & WarnAbout::NoAssertions); }
6851 bool Config::warnAboutNoTests() const { return !!(m_data.warnings & WarnAbout::NoTests); }
6852 ShowDurations::OrNot Config::showDurations() const { return m_data.showDurations; }
6853 RunTests::InWhatOrder Config::runOrder() const { return m_data.runOrder; }
6854 unsigned int Config::rngSeed() const { return m_data.rngSeed; }
6855 int Config::benchmarkResolutionMultiple() const { return m_data.benchmarkResolutionMultiple; }
6856 UseColour::YesOrNo Config::useColour() const { return m_data.useColour; }
6857 bool Config::shouldDebugBreak() const { return m_data.shouldDebugBreak; }
6858 int Config::abortAfter() const { return m_data.abortAfter; }
6859 bool Config::showInvisibles() const { return m_data.showInvisibles; }
6860 Verbosity Config::verbosity() const { return m_data.verbosity; }
6861
6862 IStream const* Config::openStream() {
6863 return Catch::makeStream(m_data.outputFilename);
6864 }
6865
6866} // end namespace Catch
6867// end catch_config.cpp

6872# pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wexit-time-destructors"
6873#endif

6875// start catch_errno_guard.h
6876
6877namespace Catch {
6878
6879 class ErrnoGuard {
6880 public:
6881 ErrnoGuard();
6882 ~ErrnoGuard();
6883 private:
6884 int m_oldErrno;
6885 };
6886
6887}

6889// end catch_errno_guard.h
6890#include <sstream>
6891
6892namespace Catch {
6893 namespace {
6894
6895 struct IColourImpl {
6896 virtual ~IColourImpl() = default;
6897 virtual void use( Colour::Code _colourCode ) = 0;
6898 };
6899
6900 struct NoColourImpl : IColourImpl {
6901 void use( Colour::Code ) {}
6902
6903 static IColourImpl* instance() {
6904 static NoColourImpl s_instance;
6905 return &s_instance;
6906 }
6907 };
6908
6909 } // anon namespace
6910} // namespace Catch
6911
6912#if !defined( CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_NONE ) && !defined( CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_WINDOWS ) && !defined( CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_ANSI )
6913# ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
6914# define CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_WINDOWS
6915# else
6916# define CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_ANSI
6917# endif
6918#endif
6919
6920#if defined ( CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_WINDOWS )
6921
6922namespace Catch {
6923namespace {
6924
6925 class Win32ColourImpl : public IColourImpl {
6926 public:
6927 Win32ColourImpl() : stdoutHandle( GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE) )
6928 {
6929 CONSOLE_SCREEN_BUFFER_INFO csbiInfo;
6930 GetConsoleScreenBufferInfo( stdoutHandle, &csbiInfo );
6931 originalForegroundAttributes = csbiInfo.wAttributes & ~( BACKGROUND_GREEN | BACKGROUND_RED | BACKGROUND_BLUE | BACKGROUND_INTENSITY );
6932 originalBackgroundAttributes = csbiInfo.wAttributes & ~( FOREGROUND_GREEN | FOREGROUND_RED | FOREGROUND_BLUE | FOREGROUND_INTENSITY );
6933 }
6934
6935 virtual void use( Colour::Code _colourCode ) override {
6936 switch( _colourCode ) {
6937 case Colour::None: return setTextAttribute( originalForegroundAttributes );
6938 case Colour::White: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_GREEN | FOREGROUND_RED | FOREGROUND_BLUE );
6939 case Colour::Red: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_RED );
6940 case Colour::Green: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_GREEN );
6941 case Colour::Blue: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_BLUE );
6942 case Colour::Cyan: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_BLUE | FOREGROUND_GREEN );
6943 case Colour::Yellow: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_RED | FOREGROUND_GREEN );
6944 case Colour::Grey: return setTextAttribute( 0 );
6945
6946 case Colour::LightGrey: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_INTENSITY );
6947 case Colour::BrightRed: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_INTENSITY | FOREGROUND_RED );
6948 case Colour::BrightGreen: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_INTENSITY | FOREGROUND_GREEN );
6949 case Colour::BrightWhite: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_INTENSITY | FOREGROUND_GREEN | FOREGROUND_RED | FOREGROUND_BLUE );
6950 case Colour::BrightYellow: return setTextAttribute( FOREGROUND_INTENSITY | FOREGROUND_RED | FOREGROUND_GREEN );
6951
6952 case Colour::Bright: CATCH_INTERNAL_ERROR( "not a colour" );
6953
6954 default:
6955 CATCH_ERROR( "Unknown colour requested" );
6956 }
6957 }
6958
6959 private:
6960 void setTextAttribute( WORD _textAttribute ) {
6961 SetConsoleTextAttribute( stdoutHandle, _textAttribute | originalBackgroundAttributes );
6962 }
6963 HANDLE stdoutHandle;
6964 WORD originalForegroundAttributes;
6965 WORD originalBackgroundAttributes;
6966 };
6967
6968 IColourImpl* platformColourInstance() {
6969 static Win32ColourImpl s_instance;
6970
6971 IConfigPtr config = getCurrentContext().getConfig();
6972 UseColour::YesOrNo colourMode = config
6973 ? config->useColour()
6974 : UseColour::Auto;
6975 if( colourMode == UseColour::Auto )
6976 colourMode = UseColour::Yes;
6977 return colourMode == UseColour::Yes
6978 ? &s_instance
6979 : NoColourImpl::instance();
6980 }
6981
6982} // end anon namespace
6983} // end namespace Catch
6984
6985#elif defined( CATCH_CONFIG_COLOUR_ANSI )
6986
6987#include <unistd.h>
6988
6989namespace Catch {
6990namespace {
6991
6992 // use POSIX/ ANSI console terminal codes
6993 // Thanks to Adam Strzelecki for original contribution
6994 // (http://github.com/nanoant)
6995 // https://github.com/philsquared/Catch/pull/131
6996 class PosixColourImpl : public IColourImpl {
6997 public:
6998 virtual void use( Colour::Code _colourCode ) override {
6999 switch( _colourCode ) {
7000 case Colour::None:
7001 case Colour::White: return setColour( "[0m" );
7002 case Colour::Red: return setColour( "[0;31m" );
7003 case Colour::Green: return setColour( "[0;32m" );
7004 case Colour::Blue: return setColour( "[0;34m" );
7005 case Colour::Cyan: return setColour( "[0;36m" );
7006 case Colour::Yellow: return setColour( "[0;33m" );
7007 case Colour::Grey: return setColour( "[1;30m" );
7008
7009 case Colour::LightGrey: return setColour( "[0;37m" );
7010 case Colour::BrightRed: return setColour( "[1;31m" );
7011 case Colour::BrightGreen: return setColour( "[1;32m" );
7012 case Colour::BrightWhite: return setColour( "[1;37m" );
7013 case Colour::BrightYellow: return setColour( "[1;33m" );
7014
7015 case Colour::Bright: CATCH_INTERNAL_ERROR( "not a colour" );
7016 default: CATCH_INTERNAL_ERROR( "Unknown colour requested" );
7017 }
7018 }
7019 static IColourImpl* instance() {
7020 static PosixColourImpl s_instance;
7021 return &s_instance;
7022 }
7023
7024 private:
7025 void setColour( const char* _escapeCode ) {
7026 Catch::cout() << '\033' << _escapeCode;
7027 }
7028 };
7029
7030 bool useColourOnPlatform() {
7031 return
7032#ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_MAC
7033 !isDebuggerActive() &&
7034#endif
7035#if !(defined(__DJGPP__) && defined(__STRICT_ANSI__))
7036 isatty(STDOUT_FILENO)
7037#else
7038 false
7039#endif
7040 ;
7041 }
7042 IColourImpl* platformColourInstance() {
7043 ErrnoGuard guard;
7044 IConfigPtr config = getCurrentContext().getConfig();
7045 UseColour::YesOrNo colourMode = config
7046 ? config->useColour()
7047 : UseColour::Auto;
7048 if( colourMode == UseColour::Auto )
7049 colourMode = useColourOnPlatform()
7050 ? UseColour::Yes
7051 : UseColour::No;
7052 return colourMode == UseColour::Yes
7053 ? PosixColourImpl::instance()
7054 : NoColourImpl::instance();
7055 }
7056
7057} // end anon namespace
7058} // end namespace Catch
7059
7060#else // not Windows or ANSI ///////////////////////////////////////////////
7061
7062namespace Catch {
7063
7064 static IColourImpl* platformColourInstance() { return NoColourImpl::instance(); }
7065
7066} // end namespace Catch
7067
7068#endif // Windows/ ANSI/ None
7069
7070namespace Catch {
7071
7072 Colour::Colour( Code _colourCode ) { use( _colourCode ); }
7073 Colour::Colour( Colour&& rhs ) noexcept {
7074 m_moved = rhs.m_moved;
7075 rhs.m_moved = true;
7076 }
7077 Colour& Colour::operator=( Colour&& rhs ) noexcept {
7078 m_moved = rhs.m_moved;
7079 rhs.m_moved = true;
7080 return *this;
7081 }
7082
7083 Colour::~Colour(){ if( !m_moved ) use( None ); }
7084
7085 void Colour::use( Code _colourCode ) {
7086 static IColourImpl* impl = platformColourInstance();
7087 impl->use( _colourCode );
7088 }
7089
7090 std::ostream& operator << ( std::ostream& os, Colour const& ) {
7091 return os;
7092 }
7093
7094} // end namespace Catch
7095
7096#if defined(__clang__)
7097# pragma clang diagnostic pop
7098#endif
7099
7100// end catch_console_colour.cpp
7101// start catch_context.cpp
7102
7103namespace Catch {
7104
7105 class Context : public IMutableContext, NonCopyable {
7106
7107 public: // IContext
7108 virtual IResultCapture* getResultCapture() override {
7109 return m_resultCapture;
7110 }
7111 virtual IRunner* getRunner() override {
7112 return m_runner;
7113 }
7114
7115 virtual IConfigPtr const& getConfig() const override {
7116 return m_config;
7117 }
7118
7119 virtual ~Context() override;
7120
7121 public: // IMutableContext
7122 virtual void setResultCapture( IResultCapture* resultCapture ) override {
7123 m_resultCapture = resultCapture;
7124 }
7125 virtual void setRunner( IRunner* runner ) override {
7126 m_runner = runner;
7127 }
7128 virtual void setConfig( IConfigPtr const& config ) override {
7129 m_config = config;
7130 }
7131
7132 friend IMutableContext& getCurrentMutableContext();
7133
7134 private:
7135 IConfigPtr m_config;
7136 IRunner* m_runner = nullptr;
7137 IResultCapture* m_resultCapture = nullptr;
7138 };
7139
7140 IMutableContext *IMutableContext::currentContext = nullptr;
7141
7142 void IMutableContext::createContext()
7143 {
7144 currentContext = new Context();
7145 }
7146
7147 void cleanUpContext() {
7148 delete IMutableContext::currentContext;
7149 IMutableContext::currentContext = nullptr;
7150 }
7151 IContext::~IContext() = default;
7152 IMutableContext::~IMutableContext() = default;
7153 Context::~Context() = default;
7154}
7155// end catch_context.cpp
7156// start catch_debug_console.cpp
7157
7158// start catch_debug_console.h
7159
7160#include <string>
7161
7162namespace Catch {
7163 void writeToDebugConsole( std::string const& text );
7164}
7165
7166// end catch_debug_console.h
7167#ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
7168
7169 namespace Catch {
7170 void writeToDebugConsole( std::string const& text ) {
7171 ::OutputDebugStringA( text.c_str() );
7172 }
7173 }
7174
7175#else
7176
7177 namespace Catch {
7178 void writeToDebugConsole( std::string const& text ) {
7179 // !TBD: Need a version for Mac/ XCode and other IDEs
7180 Catch::cout() << text;
7181 }
7182 }
7183
7184#endif // Platform
7185// end catch_debug_console.cpp
7186// start catch_debugger.cpp
7187
7188#ifdef CATCH_PLATFORM_MAC
7189
7190# include <assert.h>
7191# include <stdbool.h>
7192# include <sys/types.h>
7193# include <unistd.h>
7194# include <sys/sysctl.h>
7195# include <cstddef>
7196# include <ostream>
7197
7198namespace Catch {
7199
7200 // The following function is taken directly from the following technical note:
7201 // http://developer.apple.com/library/mac/#qa/qa2004/qa1361.html
7202
7203 // Returns true if the current process is being debugged (either
7204 // running under the debugger or has a debugger attached post facto).
7205 bool isDebuggerActive(){
7206
7207 int mib[4];
7208 struct kinfo_proc info;
7209 std::size_t size;
7210
7211 // Initialize the flags so that, if sysctl fails for some bizarre
7212 // reason, we get a predictable result.
7213
7214 info.kp_proc.p_flag = 0;
7215
7216 // Initialize mib, which tells sysctl the info we want, in this case
7217 // we're looking for information about a specific process ID.
7218
7219 mib[0] = CTL_KERN;
7220 mib[1] = KERN_PROC;
7221 mib[2] = KERN_PROC_PID;
7222 mib[3] = getpid();
7223
7224 // Call sysctl.
7225
7226 size = sizeof(info);
7227 if( sysctl(mib, sizeof(mib) / sizeof(*mib), &info, &size, nullptr, 0) != 0 ) {
7228 Catch::cerr() << "\n** Call to sysctl failed - unable to determine if debugger is active **\n" << std::endl;
7229 return false;
7230 }
7231
7232 // We're being debugged if the P_TRACED flag is set.
7233
7234 return ( (info.kp_proc.p_flag & P_TRACED) != 0 );
7235 }
7236 } // namespace Catch
7237
7238#elif defined(CATCH_PLATFORM_LINUX)
7239 #include <fstream>
7240 #include <string>
7241
7242 namespace Catch{
7243 // The standard POSIX way of detecting a debugger is to attempt to
7244 // ptrace() the process, but this needs to be done from a child and not
7245 // this process itself to still allow attaching to this process later
7246 // if wanted, so is rather heavy. Under Linux we have the PID of the
7247 // "debugger" (which doesn't need to be gdb, of course, it could also
7248 // be strace, for example) in /proc/$PID/status, so just get it from
7249 // there instead.
7250 bool isDebuggerActive(){
7251 // Libstdc++ has a bug, where std::ifstream sets errno to 0
7252 // This way our users can properly assert over errno values
7253 ErrnoGuard guard;
7254 std::ifstream in("/proc/self/status");
7255 for( std::string line; std::getline(in, line); ) {
7256 static const int PREFIX_LEN = 11;
7257 if( line.compare(0, PREFIX_LEN, "TracerPid:\t") == 0 ) {
7258 // We're traced if the PID is not 0 and no other PID starts
7259 // with 0 digit, so it's enough to check for just a single
7260 // character.
7261 return line.length() > PREFIX_LEN && line[PREFIX_LEN] != '0';
7262 }
7263 }
7264
7265 return false;
7266 }
7267 } // namespace Catch
7268#elif defined(_MSC_VER)
7269 extern "C" __declspec(dllimport) int __stdcall IsDebuggerPresent();
7270 namespace Catch {
7271 bool isDebuggerActive() {
7272 return IsDebuggerPresent() != 0;
7273 }
7274 }
7275#elif defined(__MINGW32__)
7276 extern "C" __declspec(dllimport) int __stdcall IsDebuggerPresent();
7277 namespace Catch {
7278 bool isDebuggerActive() {
7279 return IsDebuggerPresent() != 0;
7280 }
7281 }
7282#else
7283 namespace Catch {
7284 bool isDebuggerActive() { return false; }
7285 }
7286#endif // Platform
7287// end catch_debugger.cpp
7288// start catch_decomposer.cpp
7289
7290namespace Catch {
7291
7292 ITransientExpression::~ITransientExpression() = default;
7293
7294 void formatReconstructedExpression( std::ostream &os, std::string const& lhs, StringRef op, std::string const& rhs ) {
7295 if( lhs.size() + rhs.size() < 40 &&
7296 lhs.find('\n') == std::string::npos &&
7297 rhs.find('\n') == std::string::npos )
7298 os << lhs << " " << op << " " << rhs;
7299 else
7300 os << lhs << "\n" << op << "\n" << rhs;
7301 }
7302}
7303// end catch_decomposer.cpp
7304// start catch_errno_guard.cpp
7305
7306#include <cerrno>
7307
7308namespace Catch {
7309 ErrnoGuard::ErrnoGuard():m_oldErrno(errno){}
7310 ErrnoGuard::~ErrnoGuard() { errno = m_oldErrno; }
7311}
7312// end catch_errno_guard.cpp
7313// start catch_exception_translator_registry.cpp
7314
7315// start catch_exception_translator_registry.h
7316
7317#include <vector>
7318#include <string>
7319#include <memory>
7320
7321namespace Catch {
7322
7323 class ExceptionTranslatorRegistry : public IExceptionTranslatorRegistry {
7324 public:
7325 ~ExceptionTranslatorRegistry();
7326 virtual void registerTranslator( const IExceptionTranslator* translator );
7327 virtual std::string translateActiveException() const override;
7328 std::string tryTranslators() const;
7329
7330 private:
7331 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IExceptionTranslator const>> m_translators;
7332 };
7333}
7334
7335// end catch_exception_translator_registry.h
7336#ifdef __OBJC__
7337#import "Foundation/Foundation.h"
7338#endif
7339
7340namespace Catch {
7341
7342 ExceptionTranslatorRegistry::~ExceptionTranslatorRegistry() {
7343 }
7344
7345 void ExceptionTranslatorRegistry::registerTranslator( const IExceptionTranslator* translator ) {
7346 m_translators.push_back( std::unique_ptr<const IExceptionTranslator>( translator ) );
7347 }
7348
7349 std::string ExceptionTranslatorRegistry::translateActiveException() const {
7350 try {
7351#ifdef __OBJC__
7352 // In Objective-C try objective-c exceptions first
7353 @try {
7354 return tryTranslators();
7355 }
7356 @catch (NSException *exception) {
7357 return Catch::Detail::stringify( [exception description] );
7358 }
7359#else
7360 // Compiling a mixed mode project with MSVC means that CLR
7361 // exceptions will be caught in (...) as well. However, these
7362 // do not fill-in std::current_exception and thus lead to crash
7363 // when attempting rethrow.
7364 // /EHa switch also causes structured exceptions to be caught
7365 // here, but they fill-in current_exception properly, so
7366 // at worst the output should be a little weird, instead of
7367 // causing a crash.
7368 if (std::current_exception() == nullptr) {
7369 return "Non C++ exception. Possibly a CLR exception.";
7370 }
7371 return tryTranslators();
7372#endif
7373 }
7374 catch( TestFailureException& ) {
7375 std::rethrow_exception(std::current_exception());
7376 }
7377 catch( std::exception& ex ) {
7378 return ex.what();
7379 }
7380 catch( std::string& msg ) {
7381 return msg;
7382 }
7383 catch( const char* msg ) {
7384 return msg;
7385 }
7386 catch(...) {
7387 return "Unknown exception";
7388 }
7389 }
7390
7391 std::string ExceptionTranslatorRegistry::tryTranslators() const {
7392 if( m_translators.empty() )
7393 std::rethrow_exception(std::current_exception());
7394 else
7395 return m_translators[0]->translate( m_translators.begin()+1, m_translators.end() );
7396 }
7397}
7398// end catch_exception_translator_registry.cpp
7399// start catch_fatal_condition.cpp
7400
7401#if defined(__GNUC__)
7402# pragma GCC diagnostic push
7403# pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wmissing-field-initializers"
7404#endif
7405
7406#if defined( CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH ) || defined( CATCH_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS )
7407
7408namespace {
7409 // Report the error condition
7410 void reportFatal( char const * const message ) {
7411 Catch::getCurrentContext().getResultCapture()->handleFatalErrorCondition( message );
7412 }
7413}
7414
7415#endif // signals/SEH handling
7416
7417#if defined( CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH )
7418
7419namespace Catch {
7420 struct SignalDefs { DWORD id; const char* name; };
7421
7422 // There is no 1-1 mapping between signals and windows exceptions.
7423 // Windows can easily distinguish between SO and SigSegV,
7424 // but SigInt, SigTerm, etc are handled differently.
7425 static SignalDefs signalDefs[] = {
7426 { EXCEPTION_ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION, "SIGILL - Illegal instruction signal" },
7427 { EXCEPTION_STACK_OVERFLOW, "SIGSEGV - Stack overflow" },
7428 { EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION, "SIGSEGV - Segmentation violation signal" },
7429 { EXCEPTION_INT_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO, "Divide by zero error" },
7430 };
7431
7432 LONG CALLBACK FatalConditionHandler::handleVectoredException(PEXCEPTION_POINTERS ExceptionInfo) {
7433 for (auto const& def : signalDefs) {
7434 if (ExceptionInfo->ExceptionRecord->ExceptionCode == def.id) {
7435 reportFatal(def.name);
7436 }
7437 }
7438 // If its not an exception we care about, pass it along.
7439 // This stops us from eating debugger breaks etc.
7440 return EXCEPTION_CONTINUE_SEARCH;
7441 }
7442
7443 FatalConditionHandler::FatalConditionHandler() {
7444 isSet = true;
7445 // 32k seems enough for Catch to handle stack overflow,
7446 // but the value was found experimentally, so there is no strong guarantee
7447 guaranteeSize = 32 * 1024;
7448 exceptionHandlerHandle = nullptr;
7449 // Register as first handler in current chain
7450 exceptionHandlerHandle = AddVectoredExceptionHandler(1, handleVectoredException);
7451 // Pass in guarantee size to be filled
7452 SetThreadStackGuarantee(&guaranteeSize);
7453 }
7454
7455 void FatalConditionHandler::reset() {
7456 if (isSet) {
7457 RemoveVectoredExceptionHandler(exceptionHandlerHandle);
7458 SetThreadStackGuarantee(&guaranteeSize);
7459 exceptionHandlerHandle = nullptr;
7460 isSet = false;
7461 }
7462 }
7463
7464 FatalConditionHandler::~FatalConditionHandler() {
7465 reset();
7466 }
7467
7468bool FatalConditionHandler::isSet = false;
7469ULONG FatalConditionHandler::guaranteeSize = 0;
7470PVOID FatalConditionHandler::exceptionHandlerHandle = nullptr;
7471
7472} // namespace Catch
7473
7474#elif defined( CATCH_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS )
7475
7476namespace Catch {
7477
7478 struct SignalDefs {
7479 int id;
7480 const char* name;
7481 };
7482
7483 // 32kb for the alternate stack seems to be sufficient. However, this value
7484 // is experimentally determined, so that's not guaranteed.
7485 constexpr static std::size_t sigStackSize = 32768 >= MINSIGSTKSZ ? 32768 : MINSIGSTKSZ;
7486
7487 static SignalDefs signalDefs[] = {
7488 { SIGINT, "SIGINT - Terminal interrupt signal" },
7489 { SIGILL, "SIGILL - Illegal instruction signal" },
7490 { SIGFPE, "SIGFPE - Floating point error signal" },
7491 { SIGSEGV, "SIGSEGV - Segmentation violation signal" },
7492 { SIGTERM, "SIGTERM - Termination request signal" },
7493 { SIGABRT, "SIGABRT - Abort (abnormal termination) signal" }
7494 };
7495
7496 void FatalConditionHandler::handleSignal( int sig ) {
7497 char const * name = "<unknown signal>";
7498 for (auto const& def : signalDefs) {
7499 if (sig == def.id) {
7500 name = def.name;
7501 break;
7502 }
7503 }
7504 reset();
7505 reportFatal(name);
7506 raise( sig );
7507 }
7508
7509 FatalConditionHandler::FatalConditionHandler() {
7510 isSet = true;
7511 stack_t sigStack;
7512 sigStack.ss_sp = altStackMem;
7513 sigStack.ss_size = sigStackSize;
7514 sigStack.ss_flags = 0;
7515 sigaltstack(&sigStack, &oldSigStack);
7516 struct sigaction sa = { };
7517
7518 sa.sa_handler = handleSignal;
7519 sa.sa_flags = SA_ONSTACK;
7520 for (std::size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(signalDefs)/sizeof(SignalDefs); ++i) {
7521 sigaction(signalDefs[i].id, &sa, &oldSigActions[i]);
7522 }
7523 }
7524
7525 FatalConditionHandler::~FatalConditionHandler() {
7526 reset();
7527 }
7528
7529 void FatalConditionHandler::reset() {
7530 if( isSet ) {
7531 // Set signals back to previous values -- hopefully nobody overwrote them in the meantime
7532 for( std::size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(signalDefs)/sizeof(SignalDefs); ++i ) {
7533 sigaction(signalDefs[i].id, &oldSigActions[i], nullptr);
7534 }
7535 // Return the old stack
7536 sigaltstack(&oldSigStack, nullptr);
7537 isSet = false;
7538 }
7539 }
7540
7541 bool FatalConditionHandler::isSet = false;
7542 struct sigaction FatalConditionHandler::oldSigActions[sizeof(signalDefs)/sizeof(SignalDefs)] = {};
7543 stack_t FatalConditionHandler::oldSigStack = {};
7544 char FatalConditionHandler::altStackMem[sigStackSize] = {};
7545
7546} // namespace Catch
7547
7548#else
7549
7550namespace Catch {
7551 void FatalConditionHandler::reset() {}
7552}
7553
7554#endif // signals/SEH handling
7555
7556#if defined(__GNUC__)
7557# pragma GCC diagnostic pop
7558#endif
7559// end catch_fatal_condition.cpp

7590namespace Catch {
7591
7592 class ListeningReporter : public IStreamingReporter {
7593 using Reporters = std::vector<IStreamingReporterPtr>;
7594 Reporters m_listeners;
7595 IStreamingReporterPtr m_reporter = nullptr;
7596 ReporterPreferences m_preferences;
7597
7598 public:
7599 ListeningReporter();
7600
7601 void addListener( IStreamingReporterPtr&& listener );
7602 void addReporter( IStreamingReporterPtr&& reporter );
7603
7604 public: // IStreamingReporter
7605
7606 ReporterPreferences getPreferences() const override;
7607
7608 void noMatchingTestCases( std::string const& spec ) override;
7609
7610 static std::set<Verbosity> getSupportedVerbosities();
7611
7612 void benchmarkStarting( BenchmarkInfo const& benchmarkInfo ) override;
7613 void benchmarkEnded( BenchmarkStats const& benchmarkStats ) override;
7614
7615 void testRunStarting( TestRunInfo const& testRunInfo ) override;
7616 void testGroupStarting( GroupInfo const& groupInfo ) override;
7617 void testCaseStarting( TestCaseInfo const& testInfo ) override;
7618 void sectionStarting( SectionInfo const& sectionInfo ) override;
7619 void assertionStarting( AssertionInfo const& assertionInfo ) override;
7620
7621 // The return value indicates if the messages buffer should be cleared:
7622 bool assertionEnded( AssertionStats const& assertionStats ) override;
7623 void sectionEnded( SectionStats const& sectionStats ) override;
7624 void testCaseEnded( TestCaseStats const& testCaseStats ) override;
7625 void testGroupEnded( TestGroupStats const& testGroupStats ) override;
7626 void testRunEnded( TestRunStats const& testRunStats ) override;
7627
7628 void skipTest( TestCaseInfo const& testInfo ) override;
7629 bool isMulti() const override;
7630
7631 };
7632
7633} // end namespace Catch
7634
7635// end catch_reporter_listening.h
7636namespace Catch {
7637
7638 ReporterConfig::ReporterConfig( IConfigPtr const& _fullConfig )
7639 : m_stream( &_fullConfig->stream() ), m_fullConfig( _fullConfig ) {}
7640
7641 ReporterConfig::ReporterConfig( IConfigPtr const& _fullConfig, std::ostream& _stream )
7642 : m_stream( &_stream ), m_fullConfig( _fullConfig ) {}
7643
7644 std::ostream& ReporterConfig::stream() const { return *m_stream; }
7645 IConfigPtr ReporterConfig::fullConfig() const { return m_fullConfig; }
7646
7647 TestRunInfo::TestRunInfo( std::string const& _name ) : name( _name ) {}
7648
7649 GroupInfo::GroupInfo( std::string const& _name,
7650 std::size_t _groupIndex,
7651 std::size_t _groupsCount )
7652 : name( _name ),
7653 groupIndex( _groupIndex ),
7654 groupsCounts( _groupsCount )
7655 {}
7656
7657 AssertionStats::AssertionStats( AssertionResult const& _assertionResult,
7658 std::vector<MessageInfo> const& _infoMessages,
7659 Totals const& _totals )
7660 : assertionResult( _assertionResult ),
7661 infoMessages( _infoMessages ),
7662 totals( _totals )
7663 {
7664 assertionResult.m_resultData.lazyExpression.m_transientExpression = _assertionResult.m_resultData.lazyExpression.m_transientExpression;
7665
7666 if( assertionResult.hasMessage() ) {
7667 // Copy message into messages list.
7668 // !TBD This should have been done earlier, somewhere
7669 MessageBuilder builder( assertionResult.getTestMacroName(), assertionResult.getSourceInfo(), assertionResult.getResultType() );
7670 builder << assertionResult.getMessage();
7671 builder.m_info.message = builder.m_stream.str();
7672
7673 infoMessages.push_back( builder.m_info );
7674 }
7675 }
7676
7677 AssertionStats::~AssertionStats() = default;
7678
7679 SectionStats::SectionStats( SectionInfo const& _sectionInfo,
7680 Counts const& _assertions,
7681 double _durationInSeconds,
7682 bool _missingAssertions )
7683 : sectionInfo( _sectionInfo ),
7684 assertions( _assertions ),
7685 durationInSeconds( _durationInSeconds ),
7686 missingAssertions( _missingAssertions )
7687 {}
7688
7689 SectionStats::~SectionStats() = default;
7690
7691 TestCaseStats::TestCaseStats( TestCaseInfo const& _testInfo,
7692 Totals const& _totals,
7693 std::string const& _stdOut,
7694 std::string const& _stdErr,
7695 bool _aborting )
7696 : testInfo( _testInfo ),
7697 totals( _totals ),
7698 stdOut( _stdOut ),
7699 stdErr( _stdErr ),
7700 aborting( _aborting )
7701 {}
7702
7703 TestCaseStats::~TestCaseStats() = default;
7704
7705 TestGroupStats::TestGroupStats( GroupInfo const& _groupInfo,
7706 Totals const& _totals,
7707 bool _aborting )
7708 : groupInfo( _groupInfo ),
7709 totals( _totals ),
7710 aborting( _aborting )
7711 {}
7712
7713 TestGroupStats::TestGroupStats( GroupInfo const& _groupInfo )
7714 : groupInfo( _groupInfo ),
7715 aborting( false )
7716 {}
7717
7718 TestGroupStats::~TestGroupStats() = default;
7719
7720 TestRunStats::TestRunStats( TestRunInfo const& _runInfo,
7721 Totals const& _totals,
7722 bool _aborting )
7723 : runInfo( _runInfo ),
7724 totals( _totals ),
7725 aborting( _aborting )
7726 {}
7727
7728 TestRunStats::~TestRunStats() = default;
7729
7730 void IStreamingReporter::fatalErrorEncountered( StringRef ) {}
7731 bool IStreamingReporter::isMulti() const { return false; }

7733 IReporterFactory::~IReporterFactory() = default;
7734 IReporterRegistry::~IReporterRegistry() = default;

7751// start catch_leak_detector.cpp
7752
7753#ifdef CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_CRTDBG
7754#include <crtdbg.h>
7755
7756namespace Catch {
7757
7758 LeakDetector::LeakDetector() {
7759 int flag = _CrtSetDbgFlag(_CRTDBG_REPORT_FLAG);
7760 flag |= _CRTDBG_LEAK_CHECK_DF;
7761 flag |= _CRTDBG_ALLOC_MEM_DF;
7762 _CrtSetDbgFlag(flag);
7763 _CrtSetReportMode(_CRT_WARN, _CRTDBG_MODE_FILE | _CRTDBG_MODE_DEBUG);
7764 _CrtSetReportFile(_CRT_WARN, _CRTDBG_FILE_STDERR);
7765 // Change this to leaking allocation's number to break there
7766 _CrtSetBreakAlloc(-1);
7767 }
7768}
7769
7770#else
7771
7772 Catch::LeakDetector::LeakDetector() {}
7773
7774#endif
7775// end catch_leak_detector.cpp
7776// start catch_list.cpp
7777
7778// start catch_list.h
7779
7780#include <set>
7781
7782namespace Catch {
7783
7784 std::size_t listTests( Config const& config );
7785
7786 std::size_t listTestsNamesOnly( Config const& config );
7787
7788 struct TagInfo {
7789 void add( std::string const& spelling );
7790 std::string all() const;
7791
7792 std::set<std::string> spellings;
7793 std::size_t count = 0;
7794 };
7795
7796 std::size_t listTags( Config const& config );
7797
7798 std::size_t listReporters( Config const& /*config*/ );
7799
7800 Option<std::size_t> list( Config const& config );
7801
7802} // end namespace Catch
7803
7804// end catch_list.h
7805// start catch_text.h
7806
7807namespace Catch {
7808 using namespace clara::TextFlow;
7809}
7810
7811// end catch_text.h
7812#include <limits>
7813#include <algorithm>
7814#include <iomanip>
7815
7816namespace Catch {
7817
7818 std::size_t listTests( Config const& config ) {
7819 TestSpec testSpec = config.testSpec();
7820 if( config.hasTestFilters() )
7821 Catch::cout() << "Matching test cases:\n";
7822 else {
7823 Catch::cout() << "All available test cases:\n";
7824 }
7825
7826 auto matchedTestCases = filterTests( getAllTestCasesSorted( config ), testSpec, config );
7827 for( auto const& testCaseInfo : matchedTestCases ) {
7828 Colour::Code colour = testCaseInfo.isHidden()
7829 ? Colour::SecondaryText
7830 : Colour::None;
7831 Colour colourGuard( colour );
7832
7833 Catch::cout() << Column( testCaseInfo.name ).initialIndent( 2 ).indent( 4 ) << "\n";
7834 if( config.verbosity() >= Verbosity::High ) {
7835 Catch::cout() << Column( Catch::Detail::stringify( testCaseInfo.lineInfo ) ).indent(4) << std::endl;
7836 std::string description = testCaseInfo.description;
7837 if( description.empty() )
7838 description = "(NO DESCRIPTION)";
7839 Catch::cout() << Column( description ).indent(4) << std::endl;
7840 }
7841 if( !testCaseInfo.tags.empty() )
7842 Catch::cout() << Column( testCaseInfo.tagsAsString() ).indent( 6 ) << "\n";
7843 }
7844
7845 if( !config.hasTestFilters() )
7846 Catch::cout() << pluralise( matchedTestCases.size(), "test case" ) << '\n' << std::endl;
7847 else
7848 Catch::cout() << pluralise( matchedTestCases.size(), "matching test case" ) << '\n' << std::endl;
7849 return matchedTestCases.size();
7850 }
7851
7852 std::size_t listTestsNamesOnly( Config const& config ) {
7853 TestSpec testSpec = config.testSpec();
7854 std::size_t matchedTests = 0;
7855 std::vector<TestCase> matchedTestCases = filterTests( getAllTestCasesSorted( config ), testSpec, config );
7856 for( auto const& testCaseInfo : matchedTestCases ) {
7857 matchedTests++;
7858 if( startsWith( testCaseInfo.name, '#' ) )
7859 Catch::cout() << '"' << testCaseInfo.name << '"';
7860 else
7861 Catch::cout() << testCaseInfo.name;
7862 if ( config.verbosity() >= Verbosity::High )
7863 Catch::cout() << "\t@" << testCaseInfo.lineInfo;
7864 Catch::cout() << std::endl;
7865 }
7866 return matchedTests;
7867 }
7868
7869 void TagInfo::add( std::string const& spelling ) {
7870 ++count;
7871 spellings.insert( spelling );
7872 }
7873
7874 std::string TagInfo::all() const {
7875 std::string out;
7876 for( auto const& spelling : spellings )
7877 out += "[" + spelling + "]";
7878 return out;
7879 }
7880
7881 std::size_t listTags( Config const& config ) {
7882 TestSpec testSpec = config.testSpec();
7883 if( config.hasTestFilters() )
7884 Catch::cout() << "Tags for matching test cases:\n";
7885 else {
7886 Catch::cout() << "All available tags:\n";
7887 }
7888
7889 std::map<std::string, TagInfo> tagCounts;
7890
7891 std::vector<TestCase> matchedTestCases = filterTests( getAllTestCasesSorted( config ), testSpec, config );
7892 for( auto const& testCase : matchedTestCases ) {
7893 for( auto const& tagName : testCase.getTestCaseInfo().tags ) {
7894 std::string lcaseTagName = toLower( tagName );
7895 auto countIt = tagCounts.find( lcaseTagName );
7896 if( countIt == tagCounts.end() )
